---

## ESTIMATED TIMELINE

- **Phase 1 (Ingestion):** 2–3 weeks | 6 pipelines, daily + weekly schedules
- **Phase 2 (Silver/Cleansing):** 3–4 weeks | 6 staging/dimension tables, data quality rules
- **Phase 3 (Gold/Modelling):** 2–3 weeks | 5 fact/aggregate tables, complex joins
- **Phase 4 (BI/Reporting):** 2–3 weeks | 5 Power BI reports, semantic model
- **Phase 5 (Testing/Go-Live):** 2–3 weeks | QA, UAT, knowledge transfer, production cutover
- **Total:** ~12–16 weeks (3–4 months) for a team of 4–5 (1 ingestion engineer, 2 data engineers, 1 BI developer, 1 QA/PM)

---

This roadmap is sequential, traceable, and aligned with Microsoft Fabric's medallion architecture. Each story includes acceptance criteria that can be validated during sprint reviews.

sonnet-4-6.text:
# Microsoft Fabric Analytics Platform — Delivery Roadmap

---

## Architecture at a Glance

| Medallion Layer | Fabric Asset | Primary Tooling | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Bronze** (raw) | Lakehouse `lh_bronze` | Data Pipelines — Copy Activity | Immutable source landing; full audit trail |
| **Silver** (cleansed) | Lakehouse `lh_silver` | Spark Notebooks + Dataflow Gen2 | Validated, deduped, SCD-managed data |
| **Gold** (modelled) | Lakehouse `lh_gold` | Spark Notebooks | Star-schema dims & fact ready for analytics |
| **Serving** | Semantic Model (Direct Lake) + Power BI | Power BI Semantic Model, Reports | DAX measures, self-service & authored reports |

**Data flow:** ADLS file drops → `lh_bronze` → `lh_silver` → `lh_gold` → Semantic Model → Power BI Reports
**Orchestration:** A master Data Pipeline sequences child pipelines; Silver/Gold stages only run if Bronze succeeds.

---

## Functional-Requirement → Story Traceability Matrix

| FR | Description | Delivered by Stories |
|---|---|---|
| FR1 | Sales (units, gross, net) by store, region, category over date range | 1.2, 1.3, 2.1, 2.3, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.6, 4.1, 4.2, 5.1 |
| FR2 | Returns shown separately; net = gross − returns | 1.2, 2.1, 3.6, 4.2, 5.2 |
| FR3 | Promotion effectiveness vs comparable baseline | 1.3, 2.5, 3.5, 4.4, 5.3 |
| FR4 | Loyalty member vs guest segmentation on all measures | 1.3, 2.4, 3.4, 4.5, 5.4 |
| FR5 | YoY and WoW comparisons; ISO weeks spanning month boundaries | 3.1, 4.3, 5.1 |
| FR6 | Daily refresh; three years of history retained | 1.1, 1.2, 1.4, 2.1, 3.6 |

---

## Phase 1 — Foundation & Bronze Ingestion
### Sprint 1 · Weeks 1–2

**Phase goal:** Provision the Fabric platform and land every source system in `lh_bronze` in full-fidelity, append-only form. No transformation yet. Downstream phases are blocked until this completes.

---

### Story 1.1 — Fabric Workspace & Lakehouse Bootstrap

**As a** Platform Engineer, **I want** a Microsoft Fabric workspace provisioned with three Lakehouses (`lh_bronze`, `lh_silver`, `lh_gold`) on OneLake, **so that** all pipelines, notebooks, and models share a governed, single-copy storage foundation and each architectural layer can evolve independently.

**Medallion Layer:** All (infrastructure)
**Fabric Component(s):** Fabric Workspace, OneLake, Lakehouses (`lh_bronze`, `lh_silver`, `lh_gold`), Fabric Git Integration
**Functional Requirements:** FR6

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** Fabric workspace created on an appropriate capacity (F64+/P1+); three Lakehouses exist within it.
- **AC2:** ADLS Gen2 shortcut or Linked Service connection verified with a successful connectivity test.
- **AC3:** RBAC applied: Data Engineers → Contributor on all three Lakehouses; BI Developers → Contributor on `lh_gold` only; Report Consumers → Viewer on the Semantic Model.
- **AC4:** Git integration configured (Azure DevOps or GitHub); all pipeline and notebook artefacts version-controlled; changes require a peer PR review.
- **AC5:** A default Spark environment (Delta Lake runtime) and a development Spark pool are provisioned and smoke-tested with a `SELECT 1` notebook.

---

### Story 1.2 — Daily POS Transaction & Line File Ingestion (Bronze)

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a daily-scheduled Data Pipeline to copy `pos_transactions` and `pos_lines` files from ADLS into `lh_bronze` as Delta tables, **so that** three years of raw transactional history is available for downstream processing without ever modifying the source files.

**Medallion Layer:** Bronze
**Fabric Component(s):** Fabric Data Pipeline (Copy Activity, scheduled trigger), Lakehouse `lh_bronze` → Delta tables `bronze.pos_transactions`, `bronze.pos_lines`
**Functional Requirements:** FR1, FR2, FR4, FR6

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** Pipeline triggers daily at 02:00 UTC; it detects new date-partitioned files in ADLS via a watermark or file-listing pattern and copies only new arrivals (incremental by file date).
- **AC2:** `bronze.pos_transactions` lands with schema: `store_id STRING, txn_ts STRING, loyalty_id STRING (nullable), channel STRING, txn_id STRING, _source_file STRING, _ingested_ts TIMESTAMP`. Source schema is preserved unchanged.
- **AC3:** `bronze.pos_lines` lands with schema: `txn_id STRING, sku STRING, qty STRING, gross_amount STRING, discount_amount STRING, return_flag STRING, _source_file STRING, _ingested_ts TIMESTAMP`. All columns are raw strings; casting happens in Silver.
- **AC4:** Data is written in **append-only** mode; existing Bronze rows are never updated or deleted.
- **AC5:** Historical backfill for three full years is completed and validated; per-day row counts reconcile against source file manifests within ±0.01%.
- **AC6:** Pipeline writes run metadata (file name, rows read, rows written, status) to `bronze.pipeline_audit_log`; an alerting rule fires within 5 minutes of any pipeline failure.

---

### Story 1.3 — Reference & Master Data Bronze Ingestion

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** separate Bronze ingestion pipelines for `product_master` (weekly full extract), `store_master`, `promotions`, and `loyalty_members`, **so that** all dimensional source data is preserved alongside transactional data for accurate joins in later layers.

**Medallion Layer:** Bronze
**Fabric Component(s):** Fabric Data Pipelines (one Copy Activity per source), Lakehouse `lh_bronze` → `bronze.product_master`, `bronze.store_master`, `bronze.promotions`, `bronze.loyalty_members`
**Functional Requirements:** FR1, FR3, FR4

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `bronze.product_master` pipeline runs on Mondays at 03:00 UTC (weekly); each full extract is appended with an `_extract_date DATE` column so weekly snapshots are preserved for SCD processing.
- **AC2:** `bronze.store_master`, `bronze.promotions`, and `bronze.loyalty_members` trigger daily (after POS ingestion) with an `_ingested_ts TIMESTAMP` audit column; schemas match the data catalogue exactly.
- **AC3:** Row counts for each load are compared against a source file record count or manifest attribute; a discrepancy > 0.1% blocks downstream pipeline execution and triggers an alert.
- **AC4:** All four Bronze tables are queryable via the `lh_bronze` SQL Analytics Endpoint within 5 minutes of pipeline completion.
- **AC5:** Pipeline definitions are version-controlled; parameters (source path, target table name) are externalised so paths can change without code edits.

---

### Story 1.4 — Master Orchestration Pipeline & Operational Monitoring

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a master Data Pipeline that sequences all Bronze child pipelines and halts execution on any failure, **so that** Silver and Gold layers are never refreshed on stale or incomplete source data, maintaining daily SLA reliability (FR6).

**Medallion Layer:** Bronze (orchestration)
**Fabric Component(s):** Fabric Data Pipeline (parent pipeline with Execute Pipeline activities), Fabric Monitoring Hub, Fabric Activator (alerting)
**Functional Requirements:** FR6

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** Master pipeline executes in order: (a) POS ingestion, (b) product_master (if Monday), (c) store_master + promotions + loyalty_members. On success it signals Silver pipelines to proceed.
- **AC2:** Any Bronze child pipeline failure sets the master pipeline status to **Failed**; no Silver or Gold notebook is invoked until manual intervention or a re-run.
- **AC3:** Full pipeline run history — status, duration, start/end time, rows processed per step — is visible in the Fabric Monitoring Hub with 90-day retention.
- **AC4:** An anomaly check activity validates that each Bronze table's row count for the current day is within ±20% of a 7-day rolling average; outside this range raises an informational alert (does not block).
- **AC5:** End-to-end pipeline completes within a 90-minute SLA window so Gold tables are ready for the business day.

---

## Phase 2 — Silver Cleansing & Conformation
### Sprint 2 · Weeks 3–4

**Phase goal:** Apply data-quality rules, standardise types, deduplicate, handle late-arriving data, and implement SCD Type 2 for store history. Output lands in `lh_silver`. Phase 3 is blocked until Silver is complete and reconciled.

---

### Story 2.1 — POS Transaction & Lines Silver Cleansing

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a Spark Notebook to cleanse and validate `bronze.pos_transactions` and `bronze.pos_lines` into `silver.pos_transactions` and `silver.pos_lines`, **so that** null key violations, type mismatches, and orphaned line items are removed before any dimensional joins.

**Medallion Layer:** Silver
**Fabric Component(s):** Spark Notebook (PySpark), Lakehouse `lh_silver` → `silver.pos_transactions`, `silver.pos_lines`, `silver.quarantine_pos`
**Functional Requirements:** FR1, FR2, FR4, FR6

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `txn_ts` is cast to `TIMESTAMP`; rows with unparseable timestamps are written to `silver.quarantine_pos` with a `rejection_reason` column value of `"invalid_timestamp"`.
- **AC2:** POS lines with no matching `txn_id` in `silver.pos_transactions` are quarantined (`rejection_reason = "orphaned_line"`); referential integrity post-cleansing ≥ 99.9%.
- **AC3:** `return_flag` is cast to `BOOLEAN`; `qty`, `gross_amount`, `discount_amount` are cast to `DECIMAL(18,4)`; lines with negative amounts on non-return rows are quarantined.
- **AC4:** `loyalty_id` nulls are **preserved** (they represent guest transactions); no imputation occurs at this layer.
- **AC5:** Notebook is idempotent: re-running for any date partition produces the same Silver output (uses partition-overwrite or Delta `MERGE`).
- **AC6:** Quarantine row count is written to `bronze.pipeline_audit_log`; a quarantine rate > 0.5% for any single day triggers an alert.

---

### Story 2.2 — Store Master SCD Type 2 Processing (Silver)

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a Spark Notebook to maintain `silver.dim_store_history` as an SCD Type 2 table from `bronze.store_master`, **so that** changes to a store's region or format are tracked with effective date ranges, enabling sales to be attributed to the correct region at the time of transaction (FR1).

**Medallion Layer:** Silver
**Fabric Component(s):** Spark Notebook (Delta `MERGE` upsert pattern), Lakehouse `lh_silver` → `silver.dim_store_history`
**Functional Requirements:** FR1, FR6

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `silver.dim_store_history` schema: `store_key BIGINT (surrogate, auto-increment)`, `store_id STRING`, `store_name STRING`, `region STRING`, `format STRING`, `effective_from DATE`, `effective_to DATE (NULL = current)`, `is_current BOOLEAN`.
- **AC2:** On each daily run, new stores insert one row; changed stores close the prior row (`effective_to = today − 1`, `is_current = FALSE`) and open a new row (`effective_from = today`, `is_current = TRUE`).
- **AC3:** Unchanged stores produce no Delta writes; the Delta transaction log confirms zero spurious updates for unchanged records.
- **AC4:** Test case: a store with three simulated region changes returns three non-overlapping date-range rows summing to the store's full existence period with no gaps.
- **AC5:** All `store_id` values present in `silver.pos_transactions` over the last 3 years have at least one `is_current = TRUE` row in `silver.dim_store_history`.

---

### Story 2.3 — Product Master Silver Cleansing & Standardisation

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a Dataflow Gen2 to cleanse `bronze.product_master` into `silver.product_master`, **so that** category and brand values are consistently formatted and ready to serve as dimension attributes in Gold.

**Medallion Layer:** Silver
**Fabric Component(s):** Dataflow Gen2 (Power Query), output to Lakehouse `lh_silver` → `silver.product_master`
**Functional Requirements:** FR1, FR3

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `category` and `brand` values are trimmed of whitespace and normalised to consistent title-case; a curated lookup table rejects unrecognised category codes into a quarantine output.
- **AC2:** Duplicate `sku` rows within the same weekly extract are deduplicated (keep last); across extracts, only the most recent version by `_extract_date` is retained.
- **AC3:** No null `sku` or null `product_name` rows exist in `silver.product_master`; any such rows are quarantined.
- **AC4:** `category` domain in `silver.product_master` is validated to match the `category` domain in `silver.promotions`; mismatches are written to a data-quality report table.
- **AC5:** Dataflow refresh is triggered by the master orchestration pipeline immediately after Bronze reference data lands successfully.

---

### Story 2.4 — Loyalty Members Silver Cleansing & Guest Stub

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a Dataflow Gen2 to cleanse `bronze.loyalty_members` into `silver.loyalty_members` and upsert a synthetic `GUEST` stub record, **so that** every transaction — including those without a `loyalty_id` — maps to a valid customer record, enabling complete loyalty-vs-guest segmentation (FR4).

**Medallion Layer:** Silver
**Fabric Component(s):** Dataflow Gen2, Lakehouse `lh_silver` → `silver.loyalty_members`
**Functional Requirements:** FR4

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `silver.loyalty_members` schema: `loyalty_id STRING`, `join_date DATE`, `segment STRING`.
- **AC2:** A synthetic row `{loyalty_id: 'GUEST', join_date: 1900-01-01, segment: 'Guest'}` is upserted on every refresh and is always present.
- **AC3:** `join_date` cast to `DATE`; unparseable values quarantined.
- **AC4:** Duplicate `loyalty_id` records deduplicated by most-recent `_ingested_ts`.
- **AC5:** Non-null `loyalty_id` values in `silver.pos_transactions` are cross-referenced against `silver.loyalty_members`; unmatched IDs are flagged in a data-quality report (informational, not blocking).

---

### Story 2.5 — Promotions Silver Validation & Date-Range Enrichment

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a Dataflow Gen2 to validate `bronze.promotions` into `silver.promotions` with clean dates and category alignment, **so that** the date-range join used to resolve `promotion_key` in the Gold layer is accurate and auditable (FR3).

**Medallion Layer:** Silver
**Fabric Component(s):** Dataflow Gen2, Lakehouse `lh_silver` → `silver.promotions`
**Functional Requirements:** FR3

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `start_date` and `end_date` cast to `DATE`; rows where `end_date < start_date` are quarantined with `rejection_reason = "invalid_date_range"`.
- **AC2:** `category` values are validated against the domain in `silver.product_master`; unmatched categories flagged in the data-quality report.
- **AC3:** Overlapping promotions for the same `(category, date)` pair are detected and written to a warning log with count; they are **not** blocked here — resolution logic is deferred to Story 3.5.
- **AC4:** A computed column `promotion_duration_days INT` (= `end_date − start_date + 1`) is added.
- **AC5:** `silver.promotions` row count = Bronze row count − quarantined rows; no rows silently dropped.

## Phase 3 — Gold Dimensional Model
### Sprint 3 · Weeks 5–6

**Phase goal:** Build the complete star schema in `lh_gold`: five dimension tables and the central `fact_sales` table. The Gold Lakehouse SQL Analytics Endpoint serves as the physical foundation for the Direct Lake Semantic Model in Phase 4.

---

### Story 3.1 — dim_date: Full Date Spine with Calendar & ISO-Week Attributes

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a Spark Notebook to generate `gold.dim_date` as a complete date spine covering at least three years of history and one year forward, **so that** all time-intelligence calculations — including ISO week comparisons and weeks that span month boundaries — have a correct, fully attributed date table (FR5).

**Medallion Layer:** Gold
**Fabric Component(s):** Spark Notebook, Lakehouse `lh_gold` → `gold.dim_date`
**Functional Requirements:** FR5, FR6

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** Table covers `today − 3 years` through `today + 1 year`; one row per calendar day; `date_key INT` (format `YYYYMMDD`) is the primary key; no duplicates, no nulls.
- **AC2:** Columns include: `date_key`, `full_date DATE`, `day_of_week INT` (1 = Monday per ISO), `day_name STRING`, `week_of_year_iso INT`, `iso_year INT`, `month_num INT`, `month_name STRING`, `quarter INT`, `calendar_year INT`, `is_weekend BOOLEAN`, `first_day_of_iso_week DATE`, `last_day_of_iso_week DATE`.
- **AC3:** `iso_year` and `week_of_year_iso` follow ISO 8601 (week 1 contains the first Thursday of the year); validated with a test case: 28 Dec 2020 belongs to `iso_year = 2020`, `week_of_year_iso = 53`.
- **AC4:** `first_day_of_iso_week` and `last_day_of_iso_week` correctly span month boundaries — e.g., the ISO week containing 1 Feb 2021 returns `first_day = 2021-01-25` (Mon) and `last_day = 2021-01-31` (Sun). **Note:** this is critical for FR5.
- **AC5:** Table is a static generated table (no source dependency); it is refreshed whenever the pipeline runs past its upper boundary date.

---

### Story 3.2 — dim_store: SCD Type 2 Store Dimension (Gold)

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a Spark Notebook to publish `silver.dim_store_history` to `gold.dim_store` with a stable surrogate key, **so that** `fact_sales` can join to the historically correct store record and regional roll-ups reflect the region valid at the time of each transaction (FR1).

*CSV mapping reference:* `store_master.region → dim_store.region`

**Medallion Layer:** Gold
**Fabric Component(s):** Spark Notebook, Lakehouse `lh_gold` → `gold.dim_store`
**Functional Requirements:** FR1

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `gold.dim_store` schema: `store_key BIGINT (PK surrogate)`, `store_id STRING`, `store_name STRING`, `region STRING`, `format STRING`, `effective_from DATE`, `effective_to DATE`, `is_current BOOLEAN`.
- **AC2:** A default "Unknown Store" row (`store_key = −1`) is always present for unresolved or late-arriving store IDs.
- **AC3:** Test query: a store with exactly one region change shows two rows with non-overlapping, contiguous `effective_from`/`effective_to` ranges with no gap day.
- **AC4:** All `store_id` values active in the last 30 days of `silver.pos_transactions` have exactly one `is_current = TRUE` row.

---

### Story 3.3 — dim_product: Product Hierarchy Dimension (Gold)

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a Spark Notebook to publish `silver.product_master` to `gold.dim_product`, **so that** product category and brand hierarchies are available for slicing `fact_sales` in all reports and promotion joins.

*CSV mapping reference:* `product_master.category → dim_product.category`

**Medallion Layer:** Gold
**Fabric Component(s):** Spark Notebook (or Dataflow Gen2), Lakehouse `lh_gold` → `gold.dim_product`
**Functional Requirements:** FR1, FR3

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `gold.dim_product` schema: `product_key BIGINT (PK surrogate)`, `sku STRING`, `product_name STRING`, `category STRING`, `brand STRING`.
- **AC2:** A default "Unknown Product" row (`product_key = −1`) is always present.
- **AC3:** All `sku` values appearing in `silver.pos_lines` over the 3-year history have a matching row; coverage ≥ 99.5%; the remainder maps to `product_key = −1`.
- **AC4:** `category` domain in `gold.dim_product` exactly matches the `category` domain in `gold.dim_promotion`; a post-load assertion query confirms no orphaned promotion categories.

---

### Story 3.4 — dim_customer: Loyalty & Guest Customer Dimension (Gold)

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a Spark Notebook to publish `silver.loyalty_members` (including the Guest stub) to `gold.dim_customer`, **so that** every row in `fact_sales` has a valid customer foreign key and loyalty-vs-guest segmentation is fully supported (FR4).

**Medallion Layer:** Gold
**Fabric Component(s):** Spark Notebook (or Dataflow Gen2), Lakehouse `lh_gold` → `gold.dim_customer`
**Functional Requirements:** FR4

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `gold.dim_customer` schema: `customer_key BIGINT (PK surrogate)`, `loyalty_id STRING`, `join_date DATE`, `segment STRING`, `is_guest BOOLEAN`.
- **AC2:** Guest stub always present: `{customer_key: 0, loyalty_id: 'GUEST', is_guest: TRUE}`; `customer_key = 0` is reserved for this row exclusively.
- **AC3:** All non-null `loyalty_id` values in `silver.pos_transactions` resolve to a row in `gold.dim_customer`; null `loyalty_id` values resolve to `customer_key = 0`.
- **AC4:** `segment` contains only approved tier values; unexpected values trigger a data-quality alert.

---

### Story 3.5 — dim_promotion: Category + Date-Range Resolved Promotion Dimension (Gold)

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a Spark Notebook to publish `silver.promotions` to `gold.dim_promotion` and produce a pre-resolved `gold.promotion_calendar` bridge table, **so that** each fact row can be efficiently attributed to the correct promotion (or "No Promotion") during the fact build, avoiding an expensive runtime date-range join in the semantic model (FR3).

*CSV mapping reference:* `promotions.promo_id → dim_promotion.promotion_key (date-range join on category + txn date)`

**Medallion Layer:** Gold
**Fabric Component(s):** Spark Notebook, Lakehouse `lh_gold` → `gold.dim_promotion`, `gold.promotion_calendar`
**Functional Requirements:** FR3

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `gold.dim_promotion` schema: `promotion_key BIGINT (PK surrogate)`, `promo_id STRING`, `category STRING`, `promo_type STRING`, `start_date DATE`, `end_date DATE`, `promotion_duration_days INT`.
- **AC2:** A default "No Promotion" row (`promotion_key = −1`, `promo_id = 'NONE'`) is always present.
- **AC3:** `gold.promotion_calendar` is a bridge/lookup table: `category STRING, promo_date DATE, promotion_key BIGINT`; it is generated by expanding each promotion over its full date range cross-joined to its category.
- **AC4:** Where overlapping promotions exist for the same `(category, promo_date)`, a **documented** business rule (e.g., most-recently started promotion wins) ensures each `(category, promo_date)` pair maps to exactly one `promotion_key`; zero duplicate `(category, promo_date)` combinations exist in the bridge table.
- **AC5:** `gold.promotion_calendar` spans the full 3-year transaction history; all dates with no promotion map to `promotion_key = −1`.

---

### Story 3.6 — fact_sales: Central Star-Schema Fact Table (Gold)

**As a** Data Engineer, **I want** a Spark Notebook to assemble `gold.fact_sales` by joining `silver.pos_lines` to all five Gold dimension tables and resolving every foreign key, **so that** a single, query-ready fact table supports all six functional requirements.

*CSV mapping references:*
`pos_lines.qty → fact_sales.units` | `pos_lines.gross_amount → fact_sales.gross_amount` | `pos_lines.discount_amount → fact_sales.discount_amount` | `pos_lines.return_flag → fact_sales.is_return`

**Medallion Layer:** Gold
**Fabric Component(s):** Spark Notebook (PySpark), Lakehouse `lh_gold` → `gold.fact_sales` (Delta-partitioned by `txn_date_key`)
**Functional Requirements:** FR1, FR2, FR3, FR4, FR5, FR6

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `gold.fact_sales` schema:

| Column | Type | Derivation |
|---|---|---|
| `sales_key` | BIGINT (PK surrogate) | Auto-generated |
| `txn_date_key` | INT (FK → `dim_date`) | Cast `txn_ts` to date → YYYYMMDD |
| `store_key` | BIGINT (FK → `dim_store`) | SCD2 lookup: `store_id` + `txn_date` within effective range |
| `product_key` | BIGINT (FK → `dim_product`) | `sku` lookup |
| `customer_key` | BIGINT (FK → `dim_customer`) | `loyalty_id` (null → `0` Guest) |
| `promotion_key` | BIGINT (FK → `dim_promotion`) | `promotion_calendar` lookup on `(category, txn_date)` |
| `units` | DECIMAL(18,4) | `pos_lines.qty` |
| `gross_amount` | DECIMAL(18,4) | `pos_lines.gross_amount` |
| `discount_amount` | DECIMAL(18,4) | `pos_lines.discount_amount` |
| `is_return` | BOOLEAN | `pos_lines.return_flag` |

- **AC2:** `store_key` uses SCD2 effective-date range: `txn_date BETWEEN dim_store.effective_from AND COALESCE(dim_store.effective_to, '9999-12-31')`; all transactions resolve to a valid, non-default `store_key` for stores present in master data.
- **AC3:** No null foreign keys exist in production rows; all unmatchable keys resolve to their respective default surrogate (`−1` or `0`).
- **AC4:** Total row count = `silver.pos_lines` rows minus quarantined rows, ±0.01%.
- **AC5:** `SUM(gross_amount)` over all years reconciles with Bronze raw sum within ±£0.01.
- **AC6:** Table is Delta-partitioned by `txn_date_key`; daily incremental load inserts new partitions only; a parameterised `full_reload` flag enables complete historical reprocessing without schema change.
- **AC7:** Query performance test: `SUM(gross_amount) GROUP BY txn_date_key` over 1 full year of data completes in under 30 seconds on the target Spark cluster.

## Phase 4 — Semantic Model & DAX Layer
### Sprint 4 · Weeks 7–8

**Phase goal:** Build a certified Power BI Semantic Model in Direct Lake mode over `lh_gold`. Define all relationships and DAX measures that power Phase 5 reports. Phase 5 is blocked until the semantic model passes all measure validation tests.

---

### Story 4.1 — Direct Lake Semantic Model on Gold Lakehouse

**As a** BI Developer, **I want** a Power BI Semantic Model in Direct Lake mode connected to `lh_gold`, with all five dimension-to-fact relationships defined, **so that** reports achieve near-import query speed without data duplication or a scheduled import refresh cycle.

**Medallion Layer:** Presentation (reads Gold)
**Fabric Component(s):** Power BI Semantic Model (Direct Lake mode), `lh_gold` SQL Analytics Endpoint
**Functional Requirements:** FR1, FR6

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** Semantic Model created in the same Fabric workspace as `lh_gold`; storage mode confirmed as **Direct Lake** (not Import or DirectQuery) via `INFO.STORAGETABLESTATES()`.
- **AC2:** Relationships defined and active:

| From (many) | To (one) | Direction |
|---|---|---|
| `fact_sales[txn_date_key]` | `dim_date[date_key]` | Single (fact → dim) |
| `fact_sales[store_key]` | `dim_store[store_key]` | Single |
| `fact_sales[product_key]` | `dim_product[product_key]` | Single |
| `fact_sales[customer_key]` | `dim_customer[customer_key]` | Single |
| `fact_sales[promotion_key]` | `dim_promotion[promotion_key]` | Single |

- **AC3:** No inactive relationships or ambiguous filter paths; Model View shows green relationship lines with correct cardinality indicators.
- **AC4:** Row-Level Security (RLS) role `RegionFilter` defined: `dim_store[region] = USERPRINCIPALNAME_REGION_LOOKUP()`; regional managers validated to see only their data.
- **AC5:** Smoke test: a blank Power BI report card displaying `SUM(fact_sales[gross_amount])` for all three calendar years returns non-zero values within 5 seconds on the target capacity.

---

### Story 4.2 — Core Sales & Returns DAX Measures

**As a** BI Developer, **I want** a certified set of DAX measures for gross sales, return amount, net sales, units sold, and discount amount, **so that** FR1 and FR2 are fully served across every report visual without ad-hoc recalculation.

**Medallion Layer:** Presentation
**Fabric Component(s):** Power BI Semantic Model — measure table `[_Sales KPIs]`
**Functional Requirements:** FR1, FR2

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `Gross Sales :=` `CALCULATE(SUM(fact_sales[gross_amount]), fact_sales[is_return] = FALSE)`
- **AC2:** `Return Amount :=` `CALCULATE(SUM(fact_sales[gross_amount]), fact_sales[is_return] = TRUE)`
- **AC3:** `Net Sales :=` `[Gross Sales] - [Return Amount]`
- **AC4:** `Units Sold :=` `CALCULATE(SUM(fact_sales[units]), fact_sales[is_return] = FALSE)`
- **AC5:** `Discount Amount :=` `SUM(fact_sales[discount_amount])`
- **AC6:** All five measures reside in display folder `Sales KPIs`; they are certified in the Fabric Data Hub.
- **AC7:** Cross-validation: `[Gross Sales] + [Return Amount]` equals the Bronze `SUM(gross_amount)` within ±£0.01 at full grain (no filter context).
- **AC8:** All measures respond correctly to simultaneous slicers on `dim_store[region]`, `dim_product[category]`, and `dim_date[calendar_year]` (FR1 validation).

---

### Story 4.3 — Year-on-Year & Week-on-Week Time Intelligence Measures

**As a** BI Developer, **I want** DAX measures for year-on-year and ISO week-on-week comparisons of Net Sales and Units Sold, including correct handling of weeks that span month and year boundaries, **so that** business users can perform period-comparison analysis as required by FR5.

**Medallion Layer:** Presentation
**Fabric Component(s):** Power BI Semantic Model — measure table `[_Time Intelligence]`; uses `dim_date[iso_year]`, `dim_date[week_of_year_iso]`
**Functional Requirements:** FR5

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `Net Sales LY :=` `CALCULATE([Net Sales], DATEADD(dim_date[full_date], -1, YEAR))` or equivalent custom TREATAS logic.
- **AC2:** `Net Sales YoY Δ :=` `[Net Sales] - [Net Sales LY]`; `Net Sales YoY % :=` `DIVIDE([Net Sales YoY Δ], [Net Sales LY])`.
- **AC3:** `Net Sales LW :=` calculated using `dim_date[iso_year]` / `dim_date[week_of_year_iso]` offset by 1 ISO-week; **does not** use `DATEADD(..., -7, DAY)` alone, which would break on ISO 53-week years.
- **AC4:** `Net Sales WoW Δ` and `Net Sales WoW %` measures exist using the same ISO-week prior period.
- **AC5:** Validation test: filtering to ISO week 1 of a year where week 1 starts in the prior December (e.g., Jan 2018 ISO week 1 starts 1 Jan 2018; ISO week 1 of 2019 starts 31 Dec 2018) returns correct prior-week and prior-year values without double-counting or truncation.
- **AC6:** All time-intelligence measures are in display folder `Time Intelligence`; they return `BLANK()` (not 0) when no prior-period data exists.

---

### Story 4.4 — Promotion Effectiveness DAX Measures

**As a** Marketing Analyst, **I want** DAX measures comparing Net Sales during each promotion window against the equivalent-length baseline period immediately prior, **so that** I can quantify incremental uplift and evaluate promotional ROI (FR3).

**Medallion Layer:** Presentation
**Fabric Component(s):** Power BI Semantic Model — measure table `[_Promotion Analytics]`; uses `dim_promotion`, `fact_sales`
**Functional Requirements:** FR3

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `Promo Net Sales :=` `CALCULATE([Net Sales], fact_sales[promotion_key] <> -1)`
- **AC2:** `Baseline Net Sales :=` Net Sales for the same category over the `promotion_duration_days` days immediately preceding `dim_promotion[start_date]`: implemented as `CALCULATE([Net Sales], DATESBETWEEN(dim_date[full_date], MIN(dim_promotion[start_date]) - [Promo Duration Days], MIN(dim_promotion[start_date]) - 1))` scoped to the filtered category context.
- **AC3:** `Promo Uplift Δ :=` `[Promo Net Sales] - [Baseline Net Sales]`; `Promo Uplift % :=` `DIVIDE([Promo Uplift Δ], [Baseline Net Sales])`.
- **AC4:** All promotion measures respond correctly when filtered to a single `promo_id`, `category`, or `promo_type` in isolation.
- **AC5:** Measures return `BLANK()` when no promotion is selected in filter context — preventing misleading comparisons on non-promotion reports.
- **AC6:** Independent SQL validation of uplift against `lh_gold` matches DAX output within ±£0.01.

---

### Story 4.5 — Loyalty vs Guest Segmentation Measures

**As a** BI Developer, **I want** DAX measures that split all core KPIs by loyalty member vs guest, **so that** every report can optionally segment performance by customer type as required by FR4.

**Medallion Layer:** Presentation
**Fabric Component(s):** Power BI Semantic Model — measure table `[_Loyalty Segmentation]`; uses `dim_customer`
**Functional Requirements:** FR4

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** `Loyalty Net Sales :=` `CALCULATE([Net Sales], dim_customer[is_guest] = FALSE)`.
- **AC2:** `Guest Net Sales :=` `CALCULATE([Net Sales], dim_customer[is_guest] = TRUE)`.
- **AC3:** Equivalent segmented variants exist for `Gross Sales`, `Units Sold`, and `Return Amount`.
- **AC4:** Identity check: `[Loyalty Net Sales] + [Guest Net Sales] = [Net Sales]` for any combination of slicers; validated as a published DAX assertion query.
- **AC5:** `dim_customer[segment]` (loyalty tier) is visible as a slicer hierarchy so analysts can drill into Gold/Silver/Bronze tier performance independently.
- **AC6:** All segmentation measures reside in display folder `Loyalty Segmentation` and are certified.

---

## Phase 5 — Power BI Reports & Dashboards
### Sprint 5 · Weeks 9–10

**Phase goal:** Deliver the four stakeholder-facing reports, each drawing exclusively from the certified Semantic Model. UAT sign-off required before publication to the `Sales Analytics` Power BI App.

---

### Story 5.1 — Sales Performance Report

**As a** Regional Sales Manager, **I want** a Power BI report showing gross sales, net sales, units, and discount by store, region, and product category over a user-selected date range with YoY comparison, **so that** I can monitor trading performance, spot regional trends, and benchmark against the prior year (FR1, FR5).

**Medallion Layer:** Presentation
**Fabric Component(s):** Power BI Report published to `Sales Analytics` App
**Functional Requirements:** FR1, FR5

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** A date-range slicer (relative or absolute picker) controls all visuals across all pages; minimum range = 1 day; maximum = 3 years.
- **AC2:** **Page 1 — Summary:** KPI cards for `[Gross Sales]`, `[Net Sales]`, `[Units Sold]`, and `[Discount Amount]` each with `[Net Sales YoY %]` trend sparklines; a horizontal bar chart ranking all regions by `[Net Sales]`.
- **AC3:** **Page 2 — Breakdown:** Matrix with rows = `dim_product[category]`, columns = `dim_store[region]`, values = `[Net Sales]`; drill-down from region to store name is enabled.
- **AC4:** **Page 3 — Weekly Trend:** Line chart of `[Net Sales]` and `[Net Sales LY]` by `dim_date[week_of_year_iso]`; correct rendering when the selected date range spans a calendar year boundary (no broken week lines due to ISO year logic).
- **AC5:** RLS validated: Regional Managers see only their region across all three pages; a test user in a second region sees different totals.
- **AC6:** All visuals load within 3 seconds on the target Fabric capacity; tested via Power BI Performance Analyser.

---

### Story 5.2 — Returns Analysis Report Page

**As a** Merchandise Manager, **I want** a dedicated returns page in the Sales Performance Report showing return amount, return rate, and gross sales side-by-side by store, category, and date range, **so that** I can identify return hotspots and investigate potential product-quality issues (FR2).

**Medallion Layer:** Presentation
**Fabric Component(s):** Power BI Report — additional page within the Sales Performance Report
**Functional Requirements:** FR2

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** **Page: Returns:** KPI cards for `[Return Amount]` and `Return Rate %` (`= DIVIDE([Return Amount], [Gross Sales])`); both respond to the shared date slicer.
- **AC2:** A bar chart ranks `dim_product[category]` by `[Return Amount]`; a line chart shows weekly `Return Rate %` trend over the selected period.
- **AC3:** A detail table shows `dim_store[store_name]`, `[Gross Sales]`, `[Return Amount]`, `Return Rate %`, `[Units Sold]`; sortable by any column; exportable to Excel.
- **AC4:** The shared date slicer from Page 1 cross-filters the Returns page; gross and net figures on the same date range are numerically consistent across pages.
- **AC5:** A tooltip on each visual confirms the `is_return` filter applied, making the measure definition transparent to end users.
- **AC6:** `[Return Amount]` is displayed as a positive number clearly labelled "Returns"; it is **never** added to `[Gross Sales]` in any visual.

---

### Story 5.3 — Promotion Effectiveness Dashboard

**As a** Marketing Analyst, **I want** a standalone Power BI report showing promotional uplift in Net Sales and Units Sold versus the pre-promotion baseline, filterable by promotion, category, type, and region, **so that** I can evaluate which promotions drove incremental revenue and identify underperforming mechanics (FR3).

**Medallion Layer:** Presentation
**Fabric Component(s):** Power BI Report — separate report in `Sales Analytics` App
**Functional Requirements:** FR3

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** A promotion slicer (single/multi-select by `promo_id` or `promo_type`) controls all visuals.
- **AC2:** A comparison card visual shows `[Promo Net Sales]` and `[Baseline Net Sales]` side by side with `[Promo Uplift %]` as a large callout indicator (green positive, red negative).
- **AC3:** A line chart shows daily `[Net Sales]` with the promotion window highlighted as a shaded band and the equivalent baseline period shaded in a contrasting colour.
- **AC4:** A summary table shows each filtered promotion: `promo_id`, `category`, `promo_type`, `start_date`, `end_date`, `[Promo Net Sales]`, `[Baseline Net Sales]`, `[Promo Uplift Δ]`, `[Promo Uplift %]`; sortable and exportable.
- **AC5:** A `dim_store[region]` filter enables geographic uplift comparison.
- **AC6:** When no promotions are active in the selected date range, all promotion visuals display an informational message: *"No promotions in selected period"* using DAX `ISBLANK` and conditional visual-level formatting (not an error state).

---

### Story 5.4 — Loyalty vs Guest Comparison Report

**As a** Loyalty Programme Manager, **I want** a Power BI report comparing Net Sales, Gross Sales, Units Sold, and Return Rate between loyalty members and guest customers over any date range, filterable by region and category, **so that** I can quantify the commercial value of the loyalty programme and make evidence-based investment decisions (FR4).

**Medallion Layer:** Presentation
**Fabric Component(s):** Power BI Report — separate page in `Sales Analytics` App
**Functional Requirements:** FR4

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- **AC1:** KPI cards show `[Loyalty Net Sales]` and `[Guest Net Sales]` side by side; a donut chart shows loyalty vs guest as % of `[Net Sales]` total.
- **AC2:** A dual-line chart shows `[Loyalty Net Sales]` and `[Guest Net Sales]` by ISO week; both share the same Y-axis with a toggle bookmark to switch to a per-axis view.
- **AC3:** A `dim_customer[segment]` slicer allows filtering to individual loyalty tiers (e.g., Gold members only); selecting Guest tier hides the tier-breakdown visual with a bookmark.
- **AC4:** A scatter chart plots each store on axes `[Loyalty Net Sales]` (X) vs `[Guest Net Sales]` (Y), bubble size = `[Units Sold]`, to surface outlier stores with unusually high or low loyalty penetration.
- **AC5:** `[Loyalty Net Sales] + [Guest Net Sales] = [Net Sales]` is validated for any slicer combination using the Performance Analyser DAX output; zero discrepancy tolerance.
- **AC6:** A "Prior Year" bookmark toggle switches all KPI cards and the trend chart to compare current period vs prior year using `[Net Sales LY]`-equivalent segmented measures, satisfying the YoY requirement (FR5) within a loyalty context.

---

## Phase Sequencing & Dependency Summary

```
Phase 1 ──► Phase 2 ──► Phase 3 ──► Phase 4 ──► Phase 5
(Bronze)    (Silver)    (Gold)      (Model)     (Reports)
```

| Phase | Key Output | Hard Dependency | Unlocks |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 — Bronze Ingestion | Raw Delta tables in `lh_bronze`; audit log; orchestration pipeline | ADLS access, Fabric workspace | Phase 2 |
| 2 — Silver Cleansing | Validated data + SCD2 store history in `lh_silver` | Phase 1 complete | Phase 3 |
| 3 — Gold Star Schema | `dim_*` tables + `fact_sales` in `lh_gold` | Phase 2 complete | Phase 4 |
| 4 — Semantic Model & DAX | Certified Direct Lake model with all measures | Phase 3 complete | Phase 5 |
| 5 — Reports | Four Power BI reports published to app | Phase 4 complete + UAT sign-off | Production go-live |

> **Critical path:** Story 1.2 → Story 2.1 → Story 3.6 → Story 4.2 → Story 5.1/5.2. Every downstream phase depends on `fact_sales` being populated and reconciled before any measure can be trusted.

sonnet-5.text:
# Fabric Analytics Build — Delivery Roadmap

**Architecture pattern:** Medallion (Bronze → Silver → Gold) in a single Fabric Lakehouse, orchestrated by Data Pipelines / Dataflow Gen2, modelled in a Power BI semantic model (Direct Lake), consumed via Power BI reports.

**Phasing logic:** Ingestion → Cleansing/Conformance → Dimensional (Gold) modelling → Semantic model & Reporting → Hardening/Ops. Each phase is a deliverable sprint; later phases assume prior phases' tables exist and are trustworthy.

---

## PHASE 1 — Foundations & Bronze Ingestion (Sprint 1)

**Goal:** Land all six sources into OneLake, unmodified, on a repeatable daily schedule, with 3 years of history.

### US-101 — Lakehouse & workspace foundation
*As a* data platform engineer, *I want* a Fabric workspace with a Lakehouse and folder structure for bronze/silver/gold, *so that* all subsequent ingestion and modelling work has a governed landing zone.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Workspace, capacity, and Lakehouse (`lh_retail`) created with `bronze/`, `silver/`, `gold/` schemas/folders.
- Git integration and workspace roles (dev/test/prod) configured.
- Fabric components: Lakehouse, OneLake, Workspace. **FR trace:** enabler for FR1–FR6.

### US-102 — Ingest pos_transactions (bronze)
*As a* data engineer, *I want* a Data Pipeline that copies daily `pos_transactions` files from ADLS into `bronze.pos_transactions`, *so that* transaction headers are available for downstream modelling.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Pipeline uses Copy Activity, parameterised by date, appends daily partition.
- Bronze table preserves raw schema + `_ingest_ts`, `_source_file`.
- Idempotent/re-runnable without duplication.
- Fabric components: Data Pipeline, Lakehouse (bronze). **FR trace:** FR1, FR6.

### US-103 — Ingest pos_lines (bronze)
*As a* data engineer, *I want* daily ingestion of `pos_lines` into `bronze.pos_lines`, *so that* item-level sales/returns detail is captured.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Same pipeline pattern as US-102; linked to same trigger.
- Row counts reconciled against source file manifest.
- Fabric components: Data Pipeline, Lakehouse (bronze). **FR trace:** FR1, FR2, FR6.

### US-104 — Ingest product_master (bronze)
*As a* data engineer, *I want* weekly full-extract ingestion of `product_master`, *so that* current product attributes are available for conformance.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Pipeline overwrites `bronze.product_master` on each weekly run; prior snapshot archived for audit.
- Fabric components: Data Pipeline. **FR trace:** FR1.

### US-105 — Ingest store_master (bronze)
*As a* data engineer, *I want* to ingest `store_master`, *so that* store attributes including region are available for change tracking.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Full snapshot loaded to `bronze.store_master` with load timestamp.
- Fabric components: Data Pipeline. **FR trace:** FR1.

### US-106 — Ingest promotions and loyalty_members (bronze)
*As a* data engineer, *I want* `promotions` and `loyalty_members` loaded into bronze, *so that* promo windows and loyalty attributes are available for segmentation and effectiveness analysis.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Both tables ingested via Pipeline/Dataflow Gen2 into `bronze.promotions`, `bronze.loyalty_members`.
- Date fields validated as proper date types.
- Fabric components: Dataflow Gen2, Data Pipeline. **FR trace:** FR3, FR4.

### US-107 — Historical backfill (3 years)
*As a* data engineer, *I want* a backfill pipeline that loads 3 years of historical `pos_transactions`/`pos_lines` files, *so that* long-range and YoY reporting has full history from day one.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Backfill executes in batches without hitting capacity throttling.
- Row counts match source archive for a sampled set of dates.
- Fabric components: Data Pipeline (metadata-driven loop). **FR trace:** FR5, FR6.

### US-108 — Orchestration & daily scheduling
*As a* data platform engineer, *I want* a master pipeline that triggers bronze ingestion daily and logs success/failure, *so that* the platform meets the daily refresh SLA.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Master pipeline runs on daily trigger; failures raise alerts (email/Teams).
- Run history retained and visible in monitoring hub.
- Fabric components: Data Pipeline, Monitor Hub. **FR trace:** FR6.

---

## PHASE 2 — Silver: Cleansing & Conformance (Sprint 2)

**Goal:** Standardise, deduplicate, type-cast, and apply business rules (SCD handling, promo date-range prep) so gold modelling starts from trusted data.

### US-201 — Cleanse pos_transactions & pos_lines
*As a* data engineer, *I want* Dataflow Gen2/notebook logic to dedupe, type-cast, and validate `pos_transactions` and `pos_lines`, *so that* the fact table is built from reliable, well-typed data.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Nulls in required fields (txn_id, store_id, sku) rejected to a quarantine table.
- `loyalty_id` nullability preserved (guest vs member distinguishable) — supports FR4.
- Duplicate txn_id+line handled via dedup rule.
- Output: `silver.pos_transactions`, `silver.pos_lines`.
- Fabric components: Notebook (PySpark) or Dataflow Gen2, Lakehouse (silver). **FR trace:** FR1, FR2, FR4, FR6.

### US-202 — Conform product_master
*As a* data engineer, *I want* `product_master` cleaned and deduplicated to one row per SKU, *so that* category/brand attributes are consistent for reporting.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Latest weekly snapshot deduped by sku; category/brand null-checked.
- Output: `silver.product_master`.
- Fabric components: Dataflow Gen2. **FR trace:** FR1.

### US-203 — Conform store_master with change history (SCD)
*As a* data engineer, *I want* store attribute changes (especially region reassignment) tracked with effective dates, *so that* historical sales report against the region valid at time of sale.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Silver table captures `effective_from`/`effective_to`/`is_current` per store_id.
- A change in `region` produces a new row rather than overwriting the old one.
- Fabric components: Notebook (SCD Type 2 logic), Lakehouse (silver). **FR trace:** FR1.

### US-204 — Conform promotions with resolvable date ranges
*As a* data engineer, *I want* `promotions` cleaned with validated non-overlapping (or explicitly flagged overlapping) start/end dates per category, *so that* promo-to-transaction date-range joins are deterministic.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Rows with null/invalid date ranges quarantined.
- Overlaps within same category flagged for business review.
- Output: `silver.promotions`.
- Fabric components: Dataflow Gen2/Notebook. **FR trace:** FR3.

### US-205 — Conform loyalty_members & derive membership flag
*As a* data engineer, *I want* `loyalty_members` cleaned and a reusable "is_member" flag/logic prepared, *so that* transactions can be segmented as member vs guest.
**Acceptance criteria**
- `silver.loyalty_members` deduped on loyalty_id; join_date validated.
- Logic confirmed: null `loyalty_id` on transaction = guest.
- Fabric components: Dataflow Gen2. **FR trace:** FR4.

### US-206 — Data quality validation suite
*As a* data quality analyst, *I want* automated DQ checks (row counts, null rates, referential integrity between pos_lines.sku and product_master, txn_id and pos_transactions) run after each silver load, *so that* bad data is caught before it reaches gold.
**Acceptance criteria**
- DQ notebook produces a pass/fail report per run; failures block downstream pipeline.
- Results logged to a `silver.dq_log` table.
- Fabric components: Notebook, Data Pipeline (conditional activity). **FR trace:** FR6.

---

## PHASE 3 — Gold: Dimensional Model (Sprint 3)

**Goal:** Build the star schema (dim_date, dim_store, dim_product, dim_promotion, dim_customer, fact_sales) exactly as per the CSV mapping, ready for semantic modelling.

### US-301 — Build dim_date with fiscal/ISO week attributes
*As a* BI developer, *I want* a date dimension with calendar date, ISO week, week-start/end, month, and year attributes, *so that* week-on-week and year-on-year comparisons — including weeks spanning month boundaries — are computed consistently.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Covers full 3-year+ history plus current year.
- Includes `iso_week`, `iso_week_start_date`, `iso_week_end_date`, `fiscal_year`, `prior_year_date` (offset by 52/53 weeks and by calendar year) columns.
- A week spanning a month boundary is assigned a single, unambiguous `iso_week` key regardless of month.
- Fabric components: Notebook or Dataflow Gen2, Lakehouse (gold). **FR trace:** FR5.

### US-302 — Build dim_store (SCD2 exposed)
*As a* BI developer, *I want* `dim_store` built from `silver.store_master` with region history preserved, *so that* store/region reporting can use either "region as of sale" or "current region" as needed.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Surrogate key `store_key`, natural key `store_id`, `region`, `format`, SCD2 date bounds carried through per CSV mapping (`store_master.region -> dim_store.region`).
- Current-row view (`store_master.region` current) also exposed for "latest region" analysis.
- Fabric components: Notebook (Spark SQL/PySpark), Lakehouse (gold). **FR trace:** FR1.

### US-303 — Build dim_product
*As a* BI developer, *I want* `dim_product` with sku, product_name, category, brand, *so that* sales can be reported by product category.
**Acceptance criteria**
- One row per sku; `category` sourced per mapping `product_master.category -> dim_product.category`.
- Slowly changing attributes handled as Type 1 (overwrite) unless business says otherwise.
- Fabric components: Notebook/Dataflow Gen2. **FR trace:** FR1.

### US-304 — Build dim_customer (loyalty vs guest)
*As a* BI developer, *I want* `dim_customer` derived from `loyalty_members` plus a synthetic "GUEST" member, *so that* every transaction can join to a customer segment dimension.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Includes `loyalty_id`, `join_date`, `segment`, and a `member_type` attribute (`Member`/`Guest`).
- A single "GUEST" surrogate row exists for null loyalty_id transactions.
- Fabric components: Notebook/Dataflow Gen2. **FR trace:** FR4.

### US-305 — Build dim_promotion with category/date-range key
*As a* BI developer, *I want* `dim_promotion` with promo_id, category, promo_type, start_date, end_date, *so that* transactions can be matched to the active promotion for their category and date.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Surrogate `promotion_key`; includes a "No Promotion" default row for unmatched transactions.
- Structure supports the date-range join described in the mapping (`promotions.promo_id -> dim_promotion.promotion_key` via category + txn date).
- Fabric components: Notebook/Dataflow Gen2. **FR trace:** FR3.

### US-306 — Build fact_sales (grain: transaction line)
*As a* BI developer, *I want* `fact_sales` at pos_lines grain with units, gross_amount, discount_amount, is_return, and FKs to date/store/product/customer/promotion, *so that* all downstream measures (FR1–FR5) can be computed from one fact table.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Mapping applied exactly: `qty->units`, `gross_amount->gross_amount`, `discount_amount->discount_amount`, `return_flag->is_return`.
- `net_amount = gross_amount - discount_amount` computed with returns handled as negative or flagged rows so returns can be isolated (supports FR2).
- Store FK resolved to the store version valid on `txn_ts` (point-in-time SCD2 lookup).
- Promotion FK resolved via category + date-range join against `dim_promotion`; defaults to "No Promotion" key when no match.
- Customer FK resolved to member or "GUEST".
- Fabric components: Notebook (PySpark, point-in-time join logic), Lakehouse (gold), Data Pipeline (orchestration). **FR trace:** FR1, FR2, FR3, FR4.

### US-307 — Promotion baseline period reference table
*As a* BI developer, *I want* a gold table/view that, for each promotion, derives a comparable non-promotion baseline window (e.g. equal-length period immediately prior, or same period prior year), *so that* promotion effectiveness can be measured against a defined baseline rather than an ad hoc one.
**Acceptance criteria**
- For each `promo_id`, table stores `promo_start`, `promo_end`, `baseline_start`, `baseline_end` using an agreed, documented rule.
- Baseline never overlaps the promotion window itself.
- Fabric components: Notebook, Lakehouse (gold). **FR trace:** FR3.

### US-308 — Gold layer orchestration & incremental refresh
*As a* data engineer, *I want* the gold build pipeline to run incrementally each day after silver completes successfully, *so that* dashboards reflect the prior day's sales without full reprocessing.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Pipeline processes only new/changed partitions (by txn date) into fact_sales; dimensions refreshed in full (small tables) or via merge/upsert (SCD2 store).
- End-to-end bronze→gold run completes within agreed nightly window.
- Fabric components: Data Pipeline, Notebook activities. **FR trace:** FR6.

---

## PHASE 4 — Semantic Model & Reporting (Sprint 4)

**Goal:** Expose the gold star schema as a governed Power BI semantic model with the specific measures FR1–FR5 require, then build reports.

### US-401 — Publish semantic model (Direct Lake)
*As a* BI developer, *I want* a Power BI semantic model built directly on the gold Lakehouse tables using Direct Lake, *so that* reports query near-real-time data without separate import refresh.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Model contains `fact_sales`, `dim_date`, `dim_store`, `dim_product`, `dim_customer`, `dim_promotion`, `promotion_baseline`.
- Relationships built on surrogate keys, correct cardinality (1:*), and role-playing date relationship (transaction date) marked as the active date table.
- Fabric components: Power BI Semantic Model (Direct Lake), Lakehouse. **FR trace:** FR1–FR5 (enabler).

### US-402 — Core sales measures (units, gross, net)
*As a* business analyst, *I want* DAX measures for Units, Gross Sales, Net Sales, *so that* I can report sales by store/region/category for any date range.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Measures: `Total Units`, `Gross Sales`, `Net Sales` = Gross − Returns, correctly slicing by `dim_store`, `dim_product.category`, and any date range via `dim_date`.
- Validated against a manual calculation for a sample store/day.
- Fabric components: Power BI Semantic Model. **FR trace:** FR1.

### US-403 — Returns-separated measures
*As a* business analyst, *I want* `Gross Returns` and `Net Sales` shown separately, with Net always reconciling to Gross − Returns, *so that* I can see return impact distinctly from gross performance.
**Acceptance criteria**
- `Gross Returns` measure isolates rows where `is_return = true`.
- Report-level test: Net = Gross − Returns for at least 3 sample slices.
- Fabric components: Power BI Semantic Model, Power BI report. **FR trace:** FR2.

### US-404 — Promotion effectiveness measures
*As a* category manager, *I want* measures comparing category sales during a promotion window against its baseline period, *so that* I can quantify promotion lift or decline.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Measures: `Promo Period Sales`, `Baseline Period Sales`, `Promo Lift %` = (Promo − Baseline)/Baseline, using `promotion_baseline` table from US-307.
- Correctly filters to the relevant category via `dim_promotion`.
- Fabric components: Power BI Semantic Model (DAX), Lakehouse. **FR trace:** FR3.

### US-405 — Loyalty vs guest segmentation measures
*As a* CRM analyst, *I want* every core measure (units, gross, net, returns) split by `member_type`, *so that* I can compare loyalty member vs guest behaviour.
**Acceptance criteria**
- All FR1/FR2 measures produce correct subtotals when sliced by `dim_customer.member_type`.
- A dedicated "Member vs Guest %" comparison visual validated against raw counts.
- Fabric components: Power BI Semantic Model. **FR trace:** FR4.

### US-406 — Time-intelligence measures (YoY, WoW, month-spanning weeks)
*As a* business analyst, *I want* year-on-year and week-on-week comparison measures that correctly handle weeks spanning month boundaries, *so that* trend reporting is accurate regardless of calendar alignment.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Measures: `Sales YoY %`, `Sales WoW %` computed using `dim_date` ISO week attributes (US-301), not native Power BI month-based time intelligence.
- Test case: a week spanning e.g. 29 Jan–4 Feb reports as one unbroken week, and its WoW comparison uses the correct prior ISO week even though it spans a different month.
- Fabric components: Power BI Semantic Model (DAX using dim_date). **FR trace:** FR5.

### US-407 — Sales Overview report page
*As a* regional manager, *I want* a report page showing units/gross/net by store, region and category with a user-selectable date range, *so that* I can review performance for any period.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Date range slicer, region/store/category filters/drill-down.
- Visuals match US-402 measures; verified against a known reference period.
- Fabric components: Power BI report. **FR trace:** FR1.

### US-408 — Returns Analysis report page
*As a* operations manager, *I want* a report page isolating returns by store/category/date, *so that* I can investigate return trends separately from sales performance.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Visuals use `Gross Returns` and return rate (% of gross) measures.
- Filters consistent with Sales Overview page (region/store/category/date).
- Fabric components: Power BI report. **FR trace:** FR2.

### US-409 — Promotion Effectiveness dashboard
*As a* category manager, *I want* a dashboard comparing promo-period vs baseline sales by category and promotion, *so that* I can decide which promotions to repeat.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Table/visual per promotion showing Promo Sales, Baseline Sales, Lift %.
- Drill-through to daily trend across promo + baseline window.
- Fabric components: Power BI report. **FR trace:** FR3.

### US-410 — Loyalty Segmentation report page
*As a* CRM analyst, *I want* a report comparing member vs guest sales, returns and units, *so that* I can assess loyalty programme impact.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Visuals split by `member_type`, with segment-level (from `dim_customer.segment`) breakdown for members.
- Consistent filters with other report pages.
- Fabric components: Power BI report. **FR trace:** FR4.

### US-411 — Trend Comparison report page (YoY/WoW)
*As a* executive stakeholder, *I want* a trend page showing WoW and YoY % change by region/category, *so that* I can quickly spot growth or decline.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Line/column visuals using US-406 measures; correctly labelled ISO week axis.
- Validated on a week that spans a month boundary and on a full-year comparison.
- Fabric components: Power BI report. **FR trace:** FR5.

---

## PHASE 5 — Operationalisation & Hardening (Sprint 5)

**Goal:** Meet the daily-refresh/3-year-retention non-functional requirement robustly and make the platform production-ready.

### US-501 — End-to-end daily refresh SLA & monitoring
*As a* platform owner, *I want* the full bronze→silver→gold→semantic model chain to run automatically every day with alerting on failure, *so that* reports are never more than one day stale.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Master pipeline schedule confirmed (e.g., 05:00 daily); Direct Lake model auto-reflects new data (or scheduled framing if needed).
- Failure alerts routed to on-call channel; retry logic configured.
- Fabric components: Data Pipeline, Monitor Hub, Semantic Model. **FR trace:** FR6.

### US-502 — Data retention & archival policy (3 years)
*As a* data platform engineer, *I want* automated lifecycle management that retains exactly 3 years of transaction history in gold/silver and archives/purges older data, *so that* storage cost is controlled while meeting the retention requirement.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Partition-based retention job removes/archives data older than 3 years (rolling) on a schedule.
- Archived data recoverable within an agreed SLA if needed.
- Fabric components: Notebook (lifecycle job), Lakehouse, Data Pipeline. **FR trace:** FR6.

### US-503 — Performance optimisation (partitioning & incremental framing)
*As a* BI developer, *I want* fact_sales partitioned by date and the semantic model tuned (Direct Lake guardrails, V-Order, appropriate table sizes), *so that* reports render quickly even over 3 years of history.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Query performance benchmark (e.g., sub-5s for standard report page) documented for a full 3-year range query.
- Fabric components: Lakehouse (partitioning, V-Order), Semantic Model. **FR trace:** FR6, FR1, FR5.

### US-504 — UAT, documentation and handover
*As a* product owner, *I want* UAT sign-off against all FR1–FR6 acceptance criteria plus a data dictionary/lineage document, *so that* the solution is verified and supportable.
**Acceptance criteria**
- Traceability matrix (FR → story → object) reviewed and signed off.
- Data lineage view available in Fabric (Lakehouse/Model lineage).
- Fabric components: Fabric lineage view, documentation repo. **FR trace:** FR1–FR6.

---

## Traceability Summary (FR → Key Stories)

| FR | Delivered by |
|---|---|
| FR1 (sales by store/region/category, date range) | US-102/103/104/105, US-201/202/203, US-302/303/306, US-402, US-407 |
| FR2 (returns vs gross, net calc) | US-103, US-201, US-306, US-403, US-408 |
| FR3 (promotion effectiveness vs baseline) | US-106, US-204, US-305, US-306, US-307, US-404, US-409 |
| FR4 (member vs guest segmentation) | US-106, US-201, US-205, US-304, US-306, US-405, US-410 |
| FR5 (YoY/WoW incl. month-spanning weeks) | US-107, US-301, US-406, US-411 |
| FR6 (daily refresh, 3-yr retention) | US-107, US-108, US-206, US-308, US-501, US-502, US-503 |

This roadmap can run as five 2-week sprints (Bronze → Silver → Gold → Semantic/Reporting → Hardening), with each phase's demo gated on the DQ/reconciliation checks embedded in its stories.

fable-5.text:
# Analytics Delivery Roadmap — Microsoft Fabric (Medallion Architecture)

## Architecture at a Glance

| Layer | Fabric Component | Content |
|---|---|---|
| **Landing/Bronze** | OneLake / Lakehouse + Data Pipelines | Raw source files, as-is, partitioned by load date |
| **Silver** | Lakehouse + Notebooks / Dataflow Gen2 | Cleansed, conformed, deduplicated, typed tables |
| **Gold** | Lakehouse (or Warehouse) + Notebooks | Star schema: `fact_sales`, `dim_store` (SCD2), `dim_product`, `dim_promotion`, `dim_customer`, `dim_date` |
| **Serving** | Semantic model (Direct Lake) + Power BI | Measures, RLS-ready model, reports |
| **Orchestration** | Data Pipelines + deployment pipelines | Daily refresh, monitoring, alerting |

---

## Phase 1 — Foundation & Bronze Ingestion

> Goal: all six sources landing reliably in OneLake with 3 years of history. *(Supports FR6 and is a prerequisite for all FRs.)*

### Story 1.1 — Workspace & Lakehouse foundation
**As a** platform engineer, **I want** a Fabric workspace with Bronze/Silver/Gold Lakehouses and a connection to the ADLS drop zone **so that** all downstream work has a governed, consistent home.
- **Fabric:** Workspace, Lakehouse ×3 (or schemas), OneLake shortcut to ADLS.
- **Traceability:** Enabler for FR1–FR6.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Bronze/Silver/Gold zones exist and are access-controlled.
  - ADLS shortcut resolves and daily drop files are visible in OneLake.
  - Dev/Test/Prod workspaces defined with a deployment pipeline.

### Story 1.2 — Ingest POS transactions and lines (Bronze)
**As a** data engineer, **I want** a Data Pipeline that lands daily `pos_transactions` and `pos_lines` files into Bronze Delta tables **so that** sales data is available for transformation each day.
- **Fabric:** Data Pipeline (Copy activity), Bronze Lakehouse, Delta tables partitioned by load date.
- **Traceability:** FR1, FR2, FR6.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Full 3-year history back-loaded; daily incremental file picked up automatically (FR6).
  - Row counts in Bronze reconcile to source files (audit log written per run).
  - Schema is stored as-is (no transformation); load metadata columns (`_ingest_ts`, `_source_file`) added.
  - Failed/late file triggers a pipeline alert.

### Story 1.3 — Ingest reference data (Bronze)
**As a** data engineer, **I want** pipelines that land `product_master` (weekly), `store_master`, `promotions`, and `loyalty_members` into Bronze **so that** dimensional context is available for modelling.
- **Fabric:** Data Pipeline, Bronze Lakehouse.
- **Traceability:** FR1, FR3, FR4 (enabler).
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Weekly product extract lands with each snapshot retained (needed for history).
  - `store_master` snapshots retained per load date to support later SCD detection.
  - All loads idempotent (re-run does not duplicate data).

---

## Phase 2 — Silver: Cleansing & Conformance

> Goal: trustworthy, typed, deduplicated tables ready for star-schema modelling.

### Story 2.1 — Cleanse and validate sales data
**As a** data engineer, **I want** a notebook that cleanses `pos_transactions`/`pos_lines` into Silver (typed columns, dedup on `txn_id`+line, null/negative checks, orphan-line quarantine) **so that** downstream measures are accurate.
- **Fabric:** Notebook (Spark), Silver Lakehouse.
- **Traceability:** FR1, FR2.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - `qty`, `gross_amount`, `discount_amount` typed as decimal/int; `return_flag` standardised to boolean (mapping per CSV spec: `return_flag → is_return`).
  - Lines with no matching transaction header are quarantined and reported, not silently dropped.
  - Duplicate transactions removed with dedup rule documented.
  - Data-quality results logged per run (row counts in/out, rejects).

### Story 2.2 — Conform reference entities
**As a** data engineer, **I want** Silver tables for product, store, promotion, and loyalty with standardised keys and values **so that** dimension builds are simple and consistent.
- **Fabric:** Dataflow Gen2 or Notebook, Silver Lakehouse.
- **Traceability:** FR1, FR3, FR4.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - `product_master` weekly snapshots merged to current view with change history preserved.
  - Store region/format values standardised; store snapshot deltas detected (input to SCD2 in Phase 3).
  - Promotion date ranges validated (`start_date <= end_date`; overlapping promos per category flagged).
  - `loyalty_id` nullability handled: null → explicit “Guest” marker (supports FR4).

---

## Phase 3 — Gold: Dimensional Model

> Goal: star schema built to the CSV mapping, supporting all analytical requirements.

### Story 3.1 — Build dim_date
**As a** BI developer, **I want** a date dimension with fiscal/ISO week, month, year, and prior-year/prior-week mapping columns **so that** YoY and WoW comparisons work correctly, including weeks spanning month boundaries.
- **Fabric:** Notebook, Gold Lakehouse.
- **Traceability:** FR1, FR5.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Covers full 3-year range + current year (FR6).
  - ISO week number and week start/end dates present; a week spanning a month boundary is a single, unbroken week (test case included) (FR5).
  - Same-week-last-year mapping column validated against known dates.

### Story 3.2 — Build dim_store with SCD Type 2
**As a** data engineer, **I want** `dim_store` maintained as SCD2 (effective-dated rows on re-region) **so that** historical sales report against the region that was valid at transaction time.
- **Fabric:** Notebook (merge logic), Gold Lakehouse.
- **Traceability:** FR1 (region reporting accuracy).
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - `store_master.region → dim_store.region` per mapping; surrogate key, `valid_from`/`valid_to`, `is_current` columns present.
  - A test store re-regioned mid-history shows sales split correctly across old/new region.
  - Daily run detects changes automatically from Silver snapshots.

### Story 3.3 — Build dim_product, dim_customer, dim_promotion
**As a** data engineer, **I want** the remaining conformed dimensions built to the mapping spec **so that** facts can be sliced by category, loyalty segment, and promotion.
- **Fabric:** Notebook / Dataflow Gen2, Gold Lakehouse.
- **Traceability:** FR1 (category), FR3 (promotion), FR4 (loyalty).
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - `product_master.category → dim_product.category` per mapping.
  - `dim_customer` includes loyalty members plus a single “Guest” member row; `segment` and `join_date` populated (FR4).
  - `dim_promotion` built with `promo_id → promotion_key`; unknown/“No promotion” member row exists.

### Story 3.4 — Build fact_sales
**As a** data engineer, **I want** `fact_sales` at receipt-line grain with all mapped measures and dimension keys, including the promotion date-range join **so that** every functional requirement can be answered from one fact.
- **Fabric:** Notebook (Spark merge, incremental daily load), Gold Lakehouse.
- **Traceability:** FR1, FR2, FR3, FR4, FR6.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Column mapping implemented exactly per CSV spec: `qty → units`, `gross_amount`, `discount_amount`, `return_flag → is_return`.
  - Promotion key resolved by **category + transaction date within promo start/end** (per mapping); non-promo lines get the “No promotion” key (FR3).
  - Store key resolved to the SCD2 row valid at `txn_ts` (FR1).
  - Customer key = loyalty member if `loyalty_id` present, else Guest (FR4).
  - Returns flagged, not netted at row level, so gross and returns are separable (FR2).
  - Daily incremental load is idempotent; 3-year reconciliation vs Silver passes within tolerance (FR6).

---

## Phase 4 — Semantic Model & Reporting

> Goal: business users self-serve against a governed model.

### Story 4.1 — Semantic model with core measures
**As a** BI developer, **I want** a Direct Lake semantic model with measures for Units, Gross Sales, Returns, Net Sales, and Discounts **so that** all reports use one consistent set of definitions.
- **Fabric:** Semantic model (Direct Lake), DAX measures.
- **Traceability:** FR1, FR2.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - `Net Sales = Gross Sales − Returns` implemented and validated against a hand-calculated sample (FR2).
  - Returns available as a standalone measure (FR2).
  - Model relationships: fact → all five dimensions, single-direction; date table marked as date table.
  - Measures reconcile to Gold totals for a known month.

### Story 4.2 — Time-intelligence measures
**As a** BI developer, **I want** YoY and WoW variants of every core measure using the ISO-week logic in `dim_date` **so that** trend comparisons are correct even across month boundaries.
- **Fabric:** Semantic model (DAX over dim_date).
- **Traceability:** FR5.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - YoY compares same ISO week last year (not same dates blindly); WoW compares prior complete week.
  - A test week spanning a month boundary returns correct WoW values (explicit test case).
  - 53-week years handled with a documented rule.

### Story 4.3 — Sales performance report
**As a** sales/regional manager, **I want** a Power BI report showing units, gross, returns, and net by store, region, and category with a free date-range slicer **so that** I can monitor performance over any period.
- **Fabric:** Power BI report on the semantic model.
- **Traceability:** FR1, FR2, FR5.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Date-range slicer supports arbitrary ranges (FR1).
  - Drill path region → store; category filter available.
  - Returns shown separately from gross; net displayed (FR2).
  - YoY/WoW toggle or visuals available (FR5).
  - Historical figures respect SCD2 regions.

### Story 4.4 — Loyalty vs guest segmentation
**As a** marketing analyst, **I want** every measure sliceable by loyalty member vs guest (and by loyalty segment) **so that** I can compare member and non-member behaviour.
- **Fabric:** Semantic model + Power BI report page.
- **Traceability:** FR4.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Member/Guest slicer works on all pages and all measures.
  - Member + Guest totals equal grand total (no leakage).
  - Segment breakdown available for members.

### Story 4.5 — Promotion effectiveness report
**As a** category manager, **I want** a report comparing category sales in a promotion window vs a comparable baseline period (e.g. equal-length pre-promo period and/or same period last year) **so that** I can quantify promotional uplift.
- **Fabric:** Power BI + DAX (dynamic baseline calculation), dim_promotion.
- **Traceability:** FR3, FR5.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Selecting a promotion shows in-window sales, baseline sales, and % uplift for the promo’s category.
  - Baseline definition documented and agreed with the business (pre-period vs same-period-LY selectable).
  - Overlapping promotions in the same category are visible/flagged, not double-counted.

---

## Phase 5 — Operationalisation & Hardening

> Goal: reliable daily service meeting the refresh and retention SLA.

### Story 5.1 — End-to-end daily orchestration
**As a** platform engineer, **I want** a master Data Pipeline that runs bronze → silver → gold → semantic model refresh daily with dependencies and failure alerts **so that** reports show yesterday’s trading each morning.
- **Fabric:** Data Pipeline (orchestration), Activator/alerts, semantic model refresh (or Direct Lake framing).
- **Traceability:** FR6.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Full chain completes before agreed business SLA; failures alert the on-call channel.
  - Partial failure does not publish inconsistent data (gold only updates on successful silver run).
  - Retry logic for late-arriving source files.

### Story 5.2 — Retention, reconciliation & data quality monitoring
**As a** data steward, **I want** automated retention (3-year rolling window), daily reconciliation checks (bronze vs gold totals), and a DQ dashboard **so that** data trust is maintained and the platform stays within scope.
- **Fabric:** Notebook (retention/vacuum), Power BI DQ dashboard, pipeline audit tables.
- **Traceability:** FR6 (retention); underpins FR1–FR5 accuracy.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Data older than 3 years is archived/purged per policy; Delta table maintenance (OPTIMIZE/VACUUM) scheduled.
  - Daily reconciliation report published; variance beyond tolerance raises an alert.
  - Quarantined records visible and trending downwards.

### Story 5.3 — UAT & release
**As a** product owner, **I want** structured UAT against the six functional requirements with signed-off test cases **so that** the solution can be released to production with confidence.
- **Fabric:** Deployment pipeline (Dev → Test → Prod).
- **Traceability:** FR1–FR6 (verification).
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - One test case minimum per FR, including the month-boundary week (FR5), a return-heavy day (FR2), and a re-regioned store (FR1).
  - Sign-off recorded; Prod deployed via deployment pipeline, not manual copy.

---

## Traceability Matrix

| FR | Delivered by stories |
|---|---|
| FR1 (sales by store/region/category, any date range) | 1.2, 2.1, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 4.1, 4.3 |
| FR2 (returns separate; net = gross − returns) | 1.2, 2.1, 3.4, 4.1, 4.3 |
| FR3 (promotion effectiveness vs baseline) | 1.3, 2.2, 3.3, 3.4, 4.5 |
| FR4 (member vs guest segmentation) | 1.3, 2.2, 3.3, 3.4, 4.4 |
| FR5 (YoY / WoW incl. month-boundary weeks) | 3.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.5 |
| FR6 (daily refresh; 3-year retention) | 1.2, 3.1, 3.4, 5.1, 5.2 |

**Sequencing rationale:** Phase 1 establishes raw data availability; Phase 2 makes it trustworthy; Phase 3 shapes it for analytics (SCD2 and the promotion date-range join are the highest-risk items, so they get dedicated stories); Phase 4 exposes it to users; Phase 5 makes it a dependable daily service.
