| **FR4 — Loyalty member versus guest segmentation** | US02, US03, US05, US06, US09, US10, US12, US13, US14 |
| **FR5 — YoY and WoW, including month-spanning weeks** | US05, US08, US09, US11, US13, US14 |
| **FR6 — Daily refresh and three years of history** | US01, US02, US03, US04, US05, US06, US07, US08, US09, US11, US15 |

kimi-k3.text:
# Fabric Analytics Delivery Roadmap — Retail Sales & Promotions

**Story key:** each story shows its ID, the standard story format, the Fabric components, the medallion layer, and the functional requirements it delivers (`Delivers:` / `Supports:`). Assumes two-week sprints; adjust cadence as needed.

## Roadmap at a glance

| Phase | Sprint(s) | Theme | Medallion layer | FRs advanced |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | 0 | Platform foundation | — | Enabler for all |
| 1 | 1–2 | Ingestion | Bronze | FR6 (underpins all) |
| 2 | 3 | Cleansing & conformance | Silver | FR1, FR2, FR3, FR4 |
| 3 | 4–5 | Star schema & semantic model | Gold | FR1–FR6 |
| 4 | 6 | Reporting | Gold → Power BI | FR1–FR5 |
| 5 | 7 | Operationalise & validate | All | FR6 (+ full UAT) |

---

## Phase 0 — Foundation (Sprint 0)

**US-01 — Workspace, Lakehouse and environments**
*As a platform engineer, I want a Fabric workspace with a schema-enabled Lakehouse (bronze/silver/gold schemas) and a dev→test→prod deployment pipeline, so that all build work happens in a governed, promotable structure.*
**Fabric:** Workspace, Lakehouse on OneLake, deployment pipelines. **Supports:** all FRs.
- Lakehouse exists with bronze/silver/gold schemas; naming standards documented.
- Deployment pipeline promotes artefacts between environments.
- Access roles set (contributors vs viewers); no personal accounts on shared artefacts.

**US-02 — Secured source connectivity**
*As a data engineer, I want a secured, read-only connection to the source ADLS container, so that pipelines can consume the daily drops without embedded credentials.*
**Fabric:** Connections/gateways, workspace identity. **Supports:** all FRs.
- Connection authenticates via workspace identity/service principal; no keys in plain text.
- Read-only access verified against the POS drop folder and extract locations.

---

## Phase 1 — Ingestion to Bronze (Sprints 1–2)

*Exit: all six sources landing reliably in OneLake as raw, auditable Delta tables.*

**US-10 — Daily POS file ingestion**
*As a data engineer, I want the daily pos_transactions and pos_lines ADLS drops copied into bronze Delta tables by a scheduled Data Pipeline, so that downstream layers always have complete, auditable raw data.*
**Fabric:** Data Pipeline (Copy activity/Copy job) → Lakehouse bronze schema. **Delivers:** FR6. **Supports:** FR1, FR2, FR4, FR5.
- Pipeline runs daily after the drop window; files appended with `source_file` and `load_ts` metadata.
- Re-running a day is idempotent (no duplicate rows).
- File-vs-bronze row counts reconciled and logged; failure raises an alert.

**US-11 — Three-year historical load**
*As a data engineer, I want the ~3 years of historical POS files bulk-loaded into bronze, so that YoY comparisons and the 3-year retention requirement can be met from day one.*
**Fabric:** Data Pipeline (bulk copy) → bronze. **Delivers:** FR6. **Supports:** FR5.
- All historical files present; counts reconciled to source manifests.
- Load is partitioned/dated so retention policies can later target >36-month data.

**US-12 — Dimension extract ingestion**
*As a data engineer, I want product_master (weekly full extract) plus store_master, promotions and loyalty_members landed in bronze with snapshot preservation, so that conformed dimensions — including the store SCD — can be built.*
**Fabric:** Data Pipelines (weekly/on-demand schedules). **Supports:** FR1, FR3, FR4.
- product_master refreshes weekly; each full snapshot retained (enables change detection for SCD2).
- store_master, promotions, loyalty_members landed with `load_ts`; counts logged.

---

## Phase 2 — Transformation to Silver (Sprint 3)

*Exit: typed, deduplicated, conformed tables; a Type-2 store dimension; data quality enforced.*

**US-20 — Cleanse and conform POS facts**
*As an analytics engineer, I want silver tables where pos_lines are typed and deduplicated and joined to their headers (txn_ts, store_id, channel, loyalty_id), with return_flag standardised to a boolean is_return, so that the gold fact is built on trusted rows.*
**Fabric:** Spark notebook (Delta MERGE). **Delivers:** FR1, FR2. **Supports:** FR4.
- Grain of one row per txn_id + sku enforced; duplicates resolved per documented rule.
- Orphan lines (no header) quarantined with a DQ report (< agreed threshold).
- Null loyalty_id preserved (drives guest classification in FR4); is_return is strictly boolean.

**US-21 — Store dimension as SCD Type 2**
*As an analytics engineer, I want store_master maintained as an effective-dated Type-2 dimension, so that sales are attributed to the region a store belonged to **at the time of each transaction** (per the store region mapping).*
**Fabric:** Spark notebook (MERGE into silver store dimension). **Delivers:** FR1. **Supports:** FR5.
- A region re-assignment closes the current row and opens a new one; no overlapping effective dates.
- A historical point-in-time query returns the correct region for a known re-regioned store.

**US-22 — Conform product, loyalty and promotions**
*As an analytics engineer, I want product, loyalty and promotion tables cleansed in silver (valid keys, valid date ranges, deduplicated), so that gold dimensions and the promotion date-range join are reliable.*
**Fabric:** Dataflow Gen2 (low-volume dims) with DQ rules. **Delivers:** FR3, FR4. **Supports:** FR1.
- sku and loyalty_id unique; promo start_date ≤ end_date enforced.
- Overlapping promotions on the same category are detected and flagged for the precedence rule (US-32).

---

## Phase 3 — Gold Star Schema & Semantic Model (Sprints 4–5)

*Exit: a governed star schema in gold, surfaced through a Direct Lake semantic model with certified measures.*

**US-30 — fact_sales and core dimensions**
*As an analytics engineer, I want a gold star schema — fact_sales at receipt-line grain with dim_store (SCD2), dim_product, dim_customer and dim_date — populated exactly per the source-to-target mapping, so that reporting has a single governed source.*
**Fabric:** Spark notebook → gold Delta tables. **Delivers:** FR1, FR2, FR4.
- Mappings applied verbatim: qty→units, gross_amount→gross_amount, discount_amount→discount_amount, return_flag→is_return; region and category resolved via dims.
- Null loyalty_id maps to a single "Guest" row in dim_customer (from loyalty_members); fact row counts tie to silver.

**US-31 — Retail calendar for YoY/WoW**
*As a BI developer, I want a dim_date with a retail week calendar (e.g. 4-4-5) whose week index runs continuously across month boundaries, plus a prior-year comparable-week mapping, so that WoW and YoY comparisons are correct even for boundary-spanning weeks.*
**Fabric:** Notebook-generated gold dim_date. **Delivers:** FR5. **Supports:** FR1.
- Every date maps to one retail week; a week spanning a month-end aggregates all 7 days in both periods.
- Prior-year week resolves via 364-day (52-week) shift; calendar covers 3 years of history + current year.

**US-32 — Promotion attribution on the fact**
*As an analytics engineer, I want each fact row resolved to a promotion_key via the specified date-range join (category + txn date within promo window), with a documented precedence rule for overlapping promos, so that promo effectiveness is measurable.*
**Fabric:** Spark notebook during gold build → dim_promotion + fact promotion_key. **Delivers:** FR3.
- promos map per spec (promo_id → promotion_key); non-promo rows carry "No promotion".
- Overlaps resolved by the agreed rule (e.g. latest start_date wins) and logged; match rate per category reported.

**US-33 — Promotion baseline windows**
*As a BI developer, I want each promotion to carry a defined comparable baseline window (default: equal-length period immediately preceding; optional year-ago), so that promo-vs-baseline comparisons are consistent across reports.*
**Fabric:** Gold promo calendar table. **Delivers:** FR3.
- Baseline start/end stored per promotion; method switchable.
- Baselines that overlap other promotions are flagged so uplift isn't contaminated.

**US-34 — Semantic model with certified measures**
*As a BI developer, I want a Direct Lake semantic model over the gold tables with DAX measures — Units, Gross Sales, Returns, Net Sales (= Gross − Returns), Discounts, YoY/WoW deltas, Member/Guest split — so that every report uses consistent, certified KPIs.*
**Fabric:** Semantic model (Direct Lake; Import + incremental refresh as fallback). **Delivers:** FR1, FR2, FR4, FR5. **Supports:** FR3, FR6.
- Net = Gross − Returns verified against source totals for sample periods (±0.01 tolerance).
- Member + Guest equals total for every measure; YoY/WoW validated on a known boundary week.

---

## Phase 4 — Reporting (Sprint 6)

*Exit: four Power BI reports/pages covering FR1–FR5, all on the certified semantic model.*

**US-40 — Sales performance report**
*As a store operations manager, I want units, gross, returns (shown separately) and net by store, region and category over any selected date range, so that I can track trading performance.*
**Fabric:** Power BI on semantic model. **Delivers:** FR1, FR2.
- Date-range slicer drives all visuals; returns displayed as a distinct measure alongside net.
- Report totals reconcile to semantic model and to a finance-approved source sample.

**US-41 — Promotion effectiveness report**
*As a category manager, I want category sales in each promotion window compared to its baseline (absolute and % uplift), so that I can judge which promotions work.*
**Fabric:** Power BI + gold promo calendar. **Delivers:** FR3.
- Uplift validated by hand-calculation on one sample promotion; drill-through to store/region works.

**US-42 — Loyalty segmentation**
*As a CRM analyst, I want every measure splittable by member vs guest (and by member segment), so that I can quantify loyalty behaviour.*
**Fabric:** Power BI + dim_customer. **Delivers:** FR4.
- Member/guest/segment slicer works on all pages; split always sums to the total.

**US-43 — Trend comparison report**
*As a commercial analyst, I want YoY and WoW trend views that handle weeks spanning month ends, so that momentum comparisons are accurate.*
**Fabric:** Power BI + dim_date retail calendar. **Delivers:** FR5.
- A selected boundary-spanning week returns 7 full days for both current and prior-year periods; % change validated on a sample.

---

## Phase 5 — Operationalise & Validate (Sprint 7)

**US-50 — Orchestrated daily refresh with monitoring**
*As a data engineer, I want the end-to-end chain (pipeline → silver → gold → semantic model) orchestrated daily with failure alerts, so that reports are current before business open and issues are caught proactively.*
**Fabric:** Pipeline orchestration (notebook/dataflow activities), Activator alerts, monitoring hub. **Delivers:** FR6.
- Dependency chain runs daily; on-time completion logged; failures alert via Teams/email.
- Documented rerun/recovery procedure.

**US-51 — Retention management**
*As a data engineer, I want retention enforced so exactly 3 years of fact history is served (older data archived per policy), so that FR6 is met without unbounded growth.*
**Fabric:** Delta partitioning, table maintenance jobs. **Delivers:** FR6. **Supports:** FR5.
- Data older than 36 months removed/archived automatically; YoY still functions across the retention boundary.

**US-52 — UAT and sign-off**
*As a business analyst, I want a UAT pack reconciling report KPIs against source extracts for agreed sample periods, so that the business signs off the numbers with confidence.*
**Delivers:** FR1–FR6 (validation).
- Each FR has a demonstrated UAT script; reconciliation within tolerance; sign-off recorded.

---

## Traceability matrix

| FR | Delivered by stories | Validated in |
|---|---|---|
| FR1 — Sales by store/region/category, any date range | US-10, 11, 12, 20, 21, 30, 34, 40 | US-40, US-52 |
| FR2 — Returns separate; net = gross − returns | US-10, 20, 30, 34, 40 | US-34, US-40 |
| FR3 — Promo window vs baseline | US-12, 22, 32, 33, 41 | US-41 |
| FR4 — Member vs guest segmentation | US-12, 20, 22, 30, 34, 42 | US-42 |
| FR5 — YoY/WoW incl. month-boundary weeks | US-11, 21, 31, 34, 43, 51 | US-43 |
| FR6 — Daily refresh; 3-yr retention | US-10, 11, 34, 50, 51 | US-50, 51, 52 |

## Key assumptions & decisions

- **Net definition:** per FR2, Net = Gross − Returns; discount_amount is surfaced as its own measure (net-after-discount can be added without re-work).
- **Calendar:** 4-4-5 retail weeks recommended for FR5; ISO-8601 possible if the business prefers — decide in Sprint 4.
- **Promo overlaps:** precedence rule (e.g. latest start_date wins) to be confirmed with the category team during US-32.
- **Grain/key:** assumes pos_lines.txn_id → pos_transactions transaction key; guests = null loyalty_id.
- **Direct Lake** preferred for the semantic model (freshness without duplicate storage); Import with incremental refresh retained as fallback.

haiku-4-5.text:
# 1. DIMENSIONAL DATA MODEL (Star Schema)

## Fact Table(s)

**FACT_TRANSACTION_LINE**
- **Grain:** One row per line item per transaction per store per day
- **Primary Key:** TransactionLineKey (or composite: TransactionKey + LineSequence)

**Measures:**
- `QuantitySold` (units)
- `GrossSalesAmount` (revenue at full price before discount)
- `DiscountAmount` (promotional or other discount applied)
- `NetSalesAmount` (revenue after discount)
- `ReturnQuantity` (units returned)
- `ReturnAmount` (value of returned items)

---

## Dimension Tables

| Dimension | Key Attributes |
|-----------|----------------|
| **DIM_STORE** | StoreKey, StoreID, StoreName, StoreFormat (high street / retail park / concession), RegionKey |
| **DIM_REGION** | RegionKey, RegionID, RegionName |
| **DIM_PRODUCT** | ProductKey, ProductID, ProductName, CategoryID, CategoryName |
| **DIM_DATE** | DateKey, CalendarDate, DayOfWeek, WeekNumber, MonthNumber, YearNumber, WeekStartDate |
| **DIM_PROMOTION** | PromotionKey, PromotionID, PromotionName, PromotionType (BOGO / PercentOff / etc.), PromotionStartDate, PromotionEndDate |
| **DIM_CUSTOMER** | CustomerKey, CustomerID, LoyaltyMemberFlag (Y/N), CustomerSegment |
| **DIM_TRANSACTION** | TransactionKey, TransactionID, TransactionDate, StoreKey, CustomerKey |

**Note on Returns & Promotions:**
- **Returns:** Captured as separate measures (`ReturnQuantity`, `ReturnAmount`) in the same fact table, allowing gross vs. net analysis without netting off
- **Promotions:** Linked via `PromotionKey` foreign key in `FACT_TRANSACTION_LINE`; enables comparison of category sales during promotion windows vs. baseline periods

---

# 2. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS

1. **The system shall enable querying of sales performance (units and revenue) filtered by any combination of store, region, product category, and date range.**

2. **The system shall disaggregate gross sales, discounts, and net sales as separate, visible line-item measures so that neither returns nor promotions are netted off.**

3. **The system shall allow comparison of category-level sales during a defined promotion window against a comparable baseline (non-promotion) period of the same calendar length.**

4. **The system shall separately report return quantities and return amounts by store, category, and date, distinct from gross sales figures.**

5. **The system shall enable segmentation of transactions and sales by loyalty member versus guest customer.**

6. **The system shall support store-level like-for-like comparison by store format (high street, retail park, concession) and by region.**

7. **The system shall support year-on-year and week-on-week sales comparison, including handling of weeks that span calendar month boundaries.**

8. **The system shall maintain a rolling three-year transaction history and refresh daily (not real-time).**

9. **The system shall provide drill-down capability from transaction-level line items to store, region, and category aggregates.**

10. **The system shall record the promotion type (e.g., BOGO, percentage-off category) and its date window, enabling promotion-level effectiveness analysis.**

sonnet-4-6.text:
# 1. Dimensional Data Model (Star Schema)

---

## Fact Table: FACT_SALES_LINE

**Grain:** One row per product line item on a transaction — the individual line printed on a receipt. A single receipt (transaction) produces multiple rows, one per product. Both sale transactions and return transactions are stored at this same grain; they are differentiated by DIM_TRANSACTION_TYPE (see note below).

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| sales_line_key | Surrogate PK | |
| date_key | FK → DIM_DATE | Date the transaction occurred |
| store_key | FK → DIM_STORE | |
| product_key | FK → DIM_PRODUCT | |
| promotion_key | FK → DIM_PROMOTION | Uses "No Promotion" surrogate row when no promotion applies; avoids nulls |
| customer_key | FK → DIM_CUSTOMER | Uses "Guest" surrogate row for non-loyalty transactions |
| transaction_type_key | FK → DIM_TRANSACTION_TYPE | Sale or Return |
| transaction_id | Degenerate dimension | Receipt/transaction number; groups lines on the same receipt |
| **units_sold** | Measure | Quantity on the line |
| **unit_list_price** | Measure | Pre-discount price per unit |
| **unit_selling_price** | Measure | Actual price per unit after discount |
| **line_revenue** | Measure | units_sold × unit_selling_price; additive |
| **discount_amount** | Measure | (unit_list_price − unit_selling_price) × units_sold; total discount on line |

**Derived measures (computed at query time):**
- Gross Sales Revenue = SUM(line_revenue) WHERE transaction_type = 'Sale'
- Return Value = SUM(line_revenue) WHERE transaction_type = 'Return'
- Net Sales Revenue = Gross Sales − Return Value

---

## Dimension Tables

### DIM_DATE
| Attribute | Notes |
|---|---|
| date_key (PK) | |
| full_date | |
| day_of_week, day_name | |
| retail_week_number | Retail/ISO week; consistent week definition regardless of month boundaries |
| week_start_date | First day of the containing retail week |
| spans_month_boundary_flag | TRUE when a retail week crosses a month boundary; supports Priya's handling requirement |
| month_number, month_name | |
| quarter_number | |
| calendar_year | |
| same_date_prior_year | Resolved date_key for year-on-year comparison |
| same_date_prior_week | Resolved date_key for week-on-week comparison |

*Three years of populated rows are required at launch.*

---

### DIM_STORE
| Attribute | Notes |
|---|---|
| store_key (PK) | |
| store_id | Natural/source key |
| store_name | |
| store_format | High Street / Retail Park / Concession; enables like-for-like comparison |
| region_name | Denormalized into store row (star schema convention) |
| estate_label | Constant rollup label (e.g., "Whole Estate") enabling full-portfolio aggregation |

*Region → Estate forms a two-level geographic hierarchy embedded in this dimension.*

---

### DIM_PRODUCT
| Attribute | Notes |
|---|---|
| product_key (PK) | |
| product_id | Natural/source key |
| product_name | |
| category | The primary slice Priya needs (e.g., "Footwear", "Accessories") |

*Sub-category was not mentioned; do not add it without further scoping.*

---

### DIM_PROMOTION
| Attribute | Notes |
|---|---|
| promotion_key (PK) | |
| promotion_id | Natural/source key |
| promotion_name | |
| promotion_type | e.g., BOGO, Percentage Off Category |
| applicable_category | Product category the promotion targets; links promotion analysis to DIM_PRODUCT |
| promotion_start_date | |
| promotion_end_date | |
| is_active_promotion | Boolean flag; FALSE on the "No Promotion" surrogate row |

**Where promotions fit:** Every fact row carries a promotion_key. Rows where no promotion was active carry the "No Promotion" surrogate. This enables the comparison of line_revenue and units_sold inside a promotion window against a baseline period with is_active_promotion = FALSE. The baseline period is user-defined at query time; it is not stored in the model.

---

### DIM_CUSTOMER
| Attribute | Notes |
|---|---|
| customer_key (PK) | |
| customer_id | Natural key; NULL for guest transactions |
| loyalty_status | 'Loyalty Member' / 'Guest' — the only segmentation Priya specified |

**Where it fits:** ~40% of transactions have a real customer_id; the remaining ~60% receive the "Guest" surrogate row. This allows any measure to be split by loyalty_status without excluding guest transactions from totals.

---

### DIM_TRANSACTION_TYPE
| Attribute | Notes |
|---|---|
| transaction_type_key (PK) | |
| transaction_type | 'Sale' / 'Return' |

**Where returns fit:** Returns are not a separate fact table; they live at the same line-item grain with transaction_type = 'Return'. This keeps gross, returns, and net derivable from a single table without joins, which was the core gap Priya described ("returns are just netted off somewhere — invisible").

---

---

# 2. Functional Requirements

**FR-01** — The system shall store sales data at the individual transaction line item level, capturing for every line: transaction identifier, transaction date, store, product, quantity sold, unit list price, unit selling price (post-discount), and the derived discount amount per line.
*(Testable: retrieve a known receipt; confirm each line is present with all six attributes populated and that discount_amount equals the arithmetic difference of prices × quantity.)*

**FR-02** — The system shall expose gross sales revenue, return value, and net sales revenue as three distinct, separately filterable and displayable measures; returns shall never be pre-netted before they reach the reporting layer.
*(Testable: identify a known return transaction; confirm it appears as a non-zero return value, that gross sales excludes it, and that net = gross − returns.)*

**FR-03** — The system shall allow users to slice units sold and revenue by any combination of store, region, and product category over any user-selected date range, including a single calendar day.
*(Testable: select one specific store, one region, one category, and a single date; confirm the result matches the sum of sourced transaction lines for those filters on that date.)*

**FR-04** — The system shall record the discount amount on each transaction line and allow it to be aggregated and filtered by store, region, product category, and date range.
*(Testable: select a promotion date range and category; confirm total discount amount matches the sum of per-line discounts from source records.)*

**FR-05** — Each transaction line shall be associated with a promotion or explicitly marked as "no promotion", with the promotion record storing: promotion name, promotion type (e.g., BOGO, Percentage Off Category), applicable product category, promotion start date, and promotion end date.
*(Testable: for a known promotion, confirm all lines transacted within its window and matching its category carry the correct promotion_key; confirm all other lines carry the "No Promotion" key.)*

**FR-06** — The system shall support a comparison of a promoted category's sales revenue and units sold during a user-defined promotion window against a user-defined baseline period of equivalent length during which no promotion was active for that category.
*(Testable: specify a known promotion window and a prior like-for-like window; confirm the system returns separate totals for each period and that the baseline period contains zero rows with an active promotion flag.)*

**FR-07** — Each store record shall carry a store-format attribute — High Street, Retail Park, or Concession — so that any metric can be filtered or grouped by format to support like-for-like comparison.
*(Testable: group revenue by store_format; confirm every store is assigned exactly one of the three valid values with no nulls.)*

**FR-08** — Store records shall include a region attribute, and regions shall roll up to a whole-estate level, enabling revenue and units to be aggregated at store, region, or whole-estate granularity within a single query.
*(Testable: sum revenue at store level, then at region level, then at estate level; confirm each level is a lossless rollup of the one below it.)*

**FR-09** — Every transaction line shall be tagged with a loyalty status of either "Loyalty Member" or "Guest"; non-loyalty transactions shall be tagged "Guest" and shall not be excluded from any aggregate totals.
*(Testable: sum all lines; confirm total equals the sum of the "Loyalty Member" subset plus the "Guest" subset with no lines unaccounted for.)*

**FR-10** — The system shall allow any sales metric (revenue, units, discount, gross, returns, net) to be segmented and compared between Loyalty Member and Guest groups.
*(Testable: filter to a single store and date range; confirm that revenue by loyalty_status = 'Loyalty Member' plus revenue by loyalty_status = 'Guest' equals total revenue for that store and range.)*

**FR-11** — The system shall hold a minimum of three full calendar years of transaction line history, accessible through the same reporting interface as current-period data.
*(Testable: query a date three years prior to the go-live date; confirm transaction line data is returned and matches source records.)*

**FR-12** — The data shall be refreshed once per calendar day; intra-day or real-time refresh is explicitly out of scope.
*(Testable: confirm that by a defined daily SLA time the fact table contains all transactions from the prior complete day; confirm no mechanism exists — nor is required — for more frequent updates.)*

**FR-13** — The date dimension shall provide year-on-year and week-on-week comparison keys so that any metric for a given period can be directly compared with the equivalent period in the prior year and the prior week without requiring end-user date arithmetic.
*(Testable: for a known date, confirm same_date_prior_year resolves to the date exactly 52 weeks or 364 days prior as defined by the chosen retail calendar convention, and same_date_prior_week resolves to seven days prior.)*

**FR-14** — The date dimension shall assign every date to a single, unambiguous retail week, such that transactions falling within a week that spans a month boundary are attributed to one defined week rather than split across two months.
*(Testable: identify a known week that crosses a month boundary; confirm all dates in that week share the same retail_week_number and that no date in that week appears in two different week groupings.)*

sonnet-5.text:
# 1. Dimensional Data Model

## Fact Table: `FACT_SALES_LINE_ITEM`

**Grain:** One row per product line, per transaction (receipt), per store, per day — i.e., the individual item scanned on a receipt. This includes both **sale** and **return** lines (distinguished by a `transaction_type` attribute), since Priya explicitly wants line-level detail and wants returns visible, not netted off silently.

**Measures (additive at this grain):**
| Measure | Notes |
|---|---|
| `quantity` | Units sold; negative (or separately signed) for return lines |
| `gross_sales_amount` | Extended price before discount |
| `discount_amount` | Amount discounted off gross — needed to quantify promotion cost ("margin given away") |
| `net_sales_amount` | gross − discount (the actual price it sold at) |
| `return_quantity` / `return_amount` | Can be derived by filtering `transaction_type = 'Return'`, or stored as explicit signed columns — either works, but **gross, returns, and net must each be independently queryable, not just netted** |

**Degenerate dimension:** `transaction_id` / `receipt_line_number` (ties lines back to a single receipt).

**Foreign Keys / Dimensions:**

- **DIM_DATE** — `date_key`, calendar date, day-of-week, ISO week number, week-start/end date, month, quarter, year, fiscal week (needed to correctly handle weeks spanning a month boundary — this dimension must encode both calendar-month and week hierarchies independently so they can be compared without forcing weeks into months).
- **DIM_STORE** — `store_key`, store name, format (high street / retail park / concession), region, estate (region rolls up to estate — a snowflake/hierarchy attribute on the store dimension, not a separate fact).
- **DIM_PRODUCT** — `product_key`, SKU, product name, category, subcategory (category is the primary level Priya wants to slice by).
- **DIM_PROMOTION** — `promotion_key`, promotion name/type (BOGO, % off category), applicable category, promotion start date, promotion end date. Nullable/"No Promotion" row for non-promo lines. This is a conformed dimension joined on the fact so promo lines and non-promo lines can both be pulled from the same fact table for baseline comparison.
- **DIM_CUSTOMER/LOYALTY** — `customer_key` (or a simple `loyalty_flag` dimension: Member vs Guest). Flagged explicitly as stretch/"cherry on top," so this can be a conformed but lower-priority dimension — even a simple degenerate flag would satisfy the stated need if a full customer dimension isn't otherwise justified by the interview.

**Where returns fit:** Returns are **not** a separate fact table per the interview — she wants them in the same trustworthy sales view, just not netted invisibly. Model them as rows in `FACT_SALES_LINE_ITEM` with `transaction_type = 'Return'`, enabling gross / returns / net to be computed by summing/filtering the same fact table rather than reconciling two separate stars.

**Where promotions fit:** Promotions are a dimension on the sales fact (each line item may or may not link to a promotion), not a separate fact. "Effectiveness" is a derived comparison: sum of `net_sales_amount`/`quantity` for lines where `promotion_key` is active for a category+window, versus the same measures for a comparable non-promotion period for that category — this is a query pattern against the one fact table, not new structure.

---

# 2. Functional Requirements

1. The system shall ingest sales data at the individual receipt-line grain, capturing quantity, gross price, discount amount, and net (post-discount) price for every item sold.
2. The system shall retain a minimum of three years of historical sales data.
3. The system shall refresh sales data on at least a daily basis (real-time is explicitly not required).
4. Users shall be able to filter and aggregate sales (units and revenue) by any user-specified date range, down to a single day.
5. Users shall be able to break down sales by product category and subcategory.
6. Users shall be able to compare sales across regions and across individual stores.
7. Users shall be able to filter/compare stores by format (high street, retail park, concession) to support like-for-like comparisons.
8. The system shall report gross sales, total returns, and net sales as three distinct, separately queryable measures at every level of aggregation (store, region, category, day).
9. The system shall capture and report discount amount given, at line-item level and aggregated by category/store/promotion/period.
10. The system shall associate each applicable transaction line with a promotion (type: BOGO, percentage-off-category, etc.) and its active date window.
11. The system shall allow users to compare category sales during a promotion window against sales for a comparable non-promotion baseline period.
12. The system shall support year-over-year comparison of sales for any selected period.
13. The system shall support week-over-week comparison of sales, including correct handling of weeks that span a calendar month boundary (i.e., week totals must not be forced to align to month boundaries).
14. (Stretch, post-launch) The system shall allow segmentation of transactions by loyalty member versus guest status.
15. At initial launch, the system must at minimum deliver: (a) sales by store/region/category over any date range, (b) promotion effectiveness versus a baseline period, and (c) returns reported separately from gross sales — per Priya's explicit "top three at launch" prioritization.

fable-5.text:
# 1. Dimensional Data Model (Star Schema)

## Fact Table: FACT_SALES_LINE

**Grain: one row per line item on a receipt (transaction), including return lines.** Priya explicitly wants "down to the individual line on a receipt," and returns must be visible rather than netted off — so return lines are carried as distinct rows (flagged via a Transaction Type indicator), never pre-netted.

**Measures:**
- Quantity (units sold; negative or separately signed for returns)
- Gross Sales Amount (price before discount × quantity)
- Discount Amount ("discount given" — she called promotions "a black hole")
- Net Sales Amount (actual selling price × quantity)
- Return Quantity / Return Amount (populated on return lines)
- Derived at query time: **Net = Gross Sales − Returns** — the model must let gross, returns, and net be reported *separately*

**Foreign keys:** Date, Store, Product, Promotion, Loyalty Status, Transaction Type. **Degenerate dimension:** Receipt/Transaction ID (needed since "each transaction has several items").

## Dimension Tables

| Dimension | Key attributes | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **DIM_DATE** | Date, day, week, month, year; week-of-year and month independently maintained | Must support YoY and WoW comparison, including **weeks that span a month boundary** (i.e., day-level grain with both week and month attributes, likely a retail calendar). 3 years of history. |
| **DIM_STORE** | Store, Store Format (high street / retail park / concession), Region | Region rolls up to whole estate. Format included for like-for-like comparison. |
| **DIM_PRODUCT** | Product, Product Category | Category is the level she cited for slicing and promotion analysis. |
| **DIM_PROMOTION** | Promotion ID, promotion type (BOGO, % off category), promotion window start/end, scope (e.g., category) | Include a **"No Promotion" default row** so non-promotional lines are identifiable — this is what enables the promo-vs-baseline comparison. |
| **DIM_LOYALTY_STATUS** | Member vs Guest indicator | She asked only to *segment* member vs guest, not for individual customer attributes — keep it to that. |
| **DIM_TRANSACTION_TYPE** | Sale vs Return | Ensures returns are separately reportable, not "netted off somewhere." |

**Where returns fit:** as fact rows at the same line-item grain, distinguished by Transaction Type, feeding separate Gross / Returns / Net measures.
**Where promotions fit:** as a dimension on the fact line, so any line can be attributed to a promotion (or "No Promotion") and category sales in a promo window can be compared to a baseline period.

*Out of scope per transcript:* cost/margin data, inventory, real-time feeds, individual customer profiles — none were requested.

---

# 2. Functional Requirements

1. The system shall store sales data at the grain of one row per receipt line item, capturing quantity and the actual selling price after discount.
2. The system shall report **units sold**, **revenue**, and **discount amount** as measures for any selected slice.
3. The system shall allow sales to be broken down by **product category**.
4. The system shall allow sales to be aggregated and compared by **store**, by **region**, and across the **whole estate** (region → estate rollup).
5. The system shall record **store format** (high street, retail park, concession) and allow filtering/comparison by format for like-for-like analysis.
6. The system shall allow users to view sales for a **single day** and for **any arbitrary date range**.
7. The system shall record which promotion (if any) applies to each sales line, including promotion type (e.g., BOGO, percentage off a category) and the promotion window.
8. The system shall support comparing sales for a product category **during a promotion window versus a user-selected comparable non-promotional period** (baseline).
9. The system shall report **gross sales**, **returns**, and **net sales** as three separately visible measures — returns must not be pre-netted into sales figures.
10. The system shall flag each transaction as **loyalty member or guest** and allow all sales and returns reporting to be segmented by that flag.
11. The system shall retain a minimum of **three years** of sales history.
12. The system shall be refreshed **daily** (data current as of the prior day; real-time is explicitly not required).
13. The system shall support **year-on-year** and **week-on-week** period comparisons, correctly handling weeks that span a month boundary.

**Launch priority (per Priya's "only three things"):** requirements 3–6 (sales by store/region/category over any date range), 7–8 (promotion effectiveness vs baseline), and 9 (returns separate from gross). Loyalty segmentation (10) is desirable but secondary ("cherry on top").

glm-5.2.text:
### 1. Dimensional Data Model (Star Schema)

**Fact Table:** `Fact_Retail_Transactions`
*   **Grain:** One row per individual line item on a receipt (transaction line).
*   **Measures:** 
    *   `Units_Sold` (Quantity)
    *   `Gross_Sales_Amount` (Price before discount)
    *   `Discount_Amount` (Value of promotion applied)
    *   `Net_Sales_Amount` (Price actually sold at after discount)
    *   `Return_Units` (Quantity returned)
    *   `Return_Amount` (Value of returns)

**Dimension Tables:**
*   `Dim_Date`: Date Key, Date, Day, Week, Month, Year, Month-Boundary-Adjusted Week Flag, Year-on-Year Comparison Key, Week-on-Week Comparison Key.
*   `Dim_Store`: Store Key, Store ID, Store Format (High Street, Retail Park, Concession), Region, Estate.
*   `Dim_Product`: Product Key, Product ID, Product Category.
*   `Dim_Promotion`: Promotion Key, Promotion ID, Promotion Type (e.g., BOGO, Percentage Off Category), Promotion Window Start Date, Promotion Window End Date.
*   `Dim_Customer`: Customer Key, Loyalty Status (Member, Guest).

**How Returns and Promotions Fit:**
*   **Returns:** Rather than netting returns off in the source system, returns are captured as separate measures (`Return_Units`, `Return_Amount`) at the transaction line grain. This allows users to aggregate and view Gross Sales, Returns, and Net Sales separately for any given day, store, or category.
*   **Promotions:** The `Dim_Promotion` table links to the fact table on the transaction line level. This allows analysts to filter sales by a specific promotion window/type and compare the resulting `Net_Sales_Amount` and `Discount_Amount` against baseline periods (where `Dim_Promotion` is null/standard) to determine if the promotion lifted sales or just eroded margin.

---

### 2. Functional Requirements

1.  **Line-Item Granularity:** The system must capture and display sales data down to the individual line item on a receipt.
2.  **Multi-Dimensional Slicing:** The system must allow users to filter and group sales data by store, region, and product category over any user-selected date range.
3.  **Sales and Discount Metrics:** The system must capture and display the quantity sold, gross revenue, discount amount, and net revenue for each transaction line item.
4.  **Promotion Effectiveness Analysis:** The system must allow users to compare sales and discount metrics for a product category during a specific promotion window against a comparable normal (baseline) period.
5.  **Store Format Segmentation:** The system must store store format (high street, retail park, concession) and allow users to filter and compare sales on a like-for-like basis by format.
6.  **Separate Returns Visibility:** The system must display gross sales, returns, and net sales as separate metrics, ensuring returns are not simply netted off and hidden from the user.
7.  **Loyalty Segmentation:** The system must allow users to segment and filter transactions by loyalty status (member versus guest).
8.  **Historical Data Retention:** The system must retain and make available at least three years of historical sales data for trend analysis.
9.  **Data Refresh Rate:** The system must refresh its data on a daily basis (real-time data is not required).
10. **Time-Based Comparisons:** The system must support year-on-year and week-on-week time comparisons, including logic to correctly handle weeks that span a month boundary.
11. **Single-Day Visibility:** The system must allow users to view sales data at a single-day granularity to investigate anomalies on specific days.

gpt-5.5.text:
## 1. Dimensional data model / star schema

### Fact table 1: `fact_receipt_line_sales`

**Grain:** one item line on one receipt/transaction at one store on one transaction date, for one product/category.  
A receipt with five items produces five fact rows. Return lines are stored at the same grain and are not netted away.

**Foreign keys / dimensional links**

- `date_key`
- `store_key`
- `product_key`
- `product_category_key`
- `promotion_key` — “No Promotion” row when no promotion applies
- `transaction_type_key` — Sale vs Return
- `loyalty_segment_key` — Member vs Guest

**Degenerate identifiers**

- `receipt_id` / `transaction_id`
- `receipt_line_number`

**Measures**

- `quantity`
- `actual_unit_price_after_discount`
- `line_revenue_after_discount`
- `discount_amount`
- `gross_sales_units` — populated for sale lines
- `gross_sales_amount` — sale amount after discount, before returns
- `return_units` — populated for return lines
- `return_amount` — return amount shown separately
- `net_units` — derived as gross units minus returned units
- `net_sales_amount` — derived as gross sales amount minus return amount

**Returns fit here:** returns are separate receipt/transaction lines with `transaction_type = Return`, separate return measures, and derived net measures. They should not be stored only as negative adjustments to sales.

**Promotions fit here:** where a sales line was affected by a promotion, it links to `dim_promotion`; discount given is captured as `discount_amount`.

---

### Fact table 2: `fact_promotion_coverage` — factless coverage table

**Grain:** one active promotion, for one calendar date, for one promoted product category.

This supports Priya’s request to compare a category during a promotion window against a normal baseline period.

**Foreign keys**

- `date_key`
- `promotion_key`
- `product_category_key`

**Measures**

- `active_promotion_flag` or `promotion_count = 1`

No sales measures live here; sales remain in `fact_receipt_line_sales`.

No store-level promotion scope is included because the transcript did not state that promotions vary by store.

---

## Dimension tables

### `dim_date`

Key attributes:

- `date_key`
- `calendar_date`
- `day_of_week`
- `week_id`
- `week_start_date`
- `week_end_date`
- `month_id`
- `month_name`
- `calendar_year`
- Prior-week / prior-year comparison attributes if used by the reporting layer

Purpose: supports single-day analysis, arbitrary date ranges, week-on-week, year-on-year, and weeks that span month boundaries.

---

### `dim_store`

Key attributes:

- `store_key`
- `store_id`
- `store_name`
- `region`
- `store_format` — high street, retail park, concession
- Estate roll-up indicator/value

Purpose: supports store, region, whole-estate, and like-for-like format comparison.

---

### `dim_product`

Key attributes:

- `product_key`
- `product_id` / SKU
- `product_name`
- `product_category_key`
- `product_category_name`

Purpose: supports receipt-line product identification and roll-up to product category.

---

### `dim_product_category`

Key attributes:

- `product_category_key`
- `product_category_name`

Purpose: conformed category dimension used for category-level reporting and promotion coverage.

---

### `dim_promotion`

Key attributes:

- `promotion_key`
- `promotion_id`
- `promotion_name`
- `promotion_type` — for example BOGO or percentage-off category
- `promotion_start_date`
- `promotion_end_date`
- `promoted_category`
- `no_promotion_flag`

Purpose: supports analysis of sales and discounts by promotion and promotion window.

---

### `dim_transaction_type`

Key attributes:

- `transaction_type_key`
- `transaction_type` — Sale or Return

Purpose: separates gross sales and returns.

---

### `dim_loyalty_segment`

Key attributes:

- `loyalty_segment_key`
- `loyalty_status` — Member or Guest

Purpose: supports member-vs-guest segmentation. Individual customer/member analysis is not included because Priya only asked for member-versus-guest segmentation.

---

### Explicitly not modeled from this transcript

- Product cost or true margin measures were not discussed, so margin is not included.
- Real-time reporting is not required.
- Store-specific promotion eligibility is not included because Priya did not mention store-specific promotions.

---

# 2. Functional requirements

1. The solution shall store sales and return data at individual receipt-line-item grain, including receipt identifier, line number, transaction date, store, product/category, quantity, actual unit price after discount, discount amount, and sale/return classification.

2. The solution shall retain three years of transaction history for trend analysis.

3. The sales data shall refresh daily; real-time refresh is not required.

4. Users shall be able to run sales reports for any selected date range, including a single calendar day.

5. Users shall be able to view units sold and revenue by store, region, and product category for any selected date range.

6. Users shall be able to compare regions and roll regions up to the whole estate.

7. Each store shall be classified by store format, and users shall be able to filter or group sales by format, including high street, retail park, and concession.

8. Reports shall calculate revenue using the actual selling price after discount.

9. Reports shall show discount amount as a separate metric that can be analyzed by date, store, region, product category, and promotion.

10. Reports shall show gross sales, returns, and net sales separately, in both units and revenue amount.

11. Return lines shall be visible by date, store, and product category so that the impact of large returns on a specific day can be identified.

12. The solution shall store promotion definitions including promotion type, promotion start date, promotion end date, and promoted product category.

13. Sales lines affected by a promotion shall be identifiable by promotion so that promoted sales and discount amounts can be analyzed.

14. The solution shall provide a promotion effectiveness view comparing sales for a selected product category during a selected promotion window against a selected normal baseline period.

15. The promotion effectiveness comparison shall show, for both the promotion period and the baseline period, units sold, revenue, discount amount, and the difference or lift between the two periods.

16. The solution shall support same-period year-on-year comparisons.

17. The solution shall support week-on-week comparisons using defined week start and end dates, including weeks that span month boundaries.

18. The solution shall allow sales metrics to be segmented by loyalty status: loyalty member versus guest.

19. For launch, the required minimum capabilities shall include sales by store, region, and product category over any date range; promotion effectiveness versus a baseline; and returns shown separately from gross sales. Loyalty segmentation is lower priority, described by Priya as “the cherry on top.”

gpt-5.6-terra.text:
## 1. Dimensional Data Model (Star Schema)

### Core fact table: `FactRetailReceiptLine`

**Grain:** One product/item line on one retail receipt/transaction, recorded as either a **sale** or a **return**.

This grain supports Priya’s stated need to analyse sales down to the individual receipt line, while still aggregating by day, store, region, format, and product category.

| Type | Field / Measure | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Foreign key | `date_key` | Date of the sale or return transaction. |
| Foreign key | `store_key` | Store where the transaction occurred. |
| Foreign key | `product_key` | Product/item sold or returned. |
| Foreign key | `loyalty_segment_key` | Identifies whether the transaction was by a loyalty member or guest. |
| Foreign key | `transaction_type_key` | Identifies the row as a sale or return. |
| Degenerate dimensions | `receipt_number`, `receipt_line_number` | Retained in the fact for receipt-level detail; no separate dimension table is necessary. |
| Fact-row identifier | `receipt_line_key` | Surrogate identifier used if a line needs to be associated with one or more promotions. |
| Measure | `quantity` | Signed quantity: positive for sales, negative for returns; alternatively stored as an absolute quantity with transaction type determining direction. |
| Measure | `actual_unit_selling_price` | The actual price at which the item was sold after discount. This is useful for line-level review but is not additive. |
| Measure | `actual_line_revenue` | Extended line revenue at the actual selling price after discount. Positive for sales and negative for returns. |
| Measure | `discount_amount` | Monetary discount given on the line. |
| Derived measure | `units_sold` | Sum of quantities for sale rows only. |
| Derived measure | `units_returned` | Absolute sum of quantities for return rows only. |
| Derived measure | `gross_sales_amount` | Sum of positive sale-line revenue, before subtracting returns. |
| Derived measure | `returns_amount` | Absolute value of return-line revenue. |
| Derived measure | `net_sales_amount` | `gross_sales_amount - returns_amount`, or equivalently the sum of signed line revenue. |

### Important sales/discount definition note

The transcript makes clear that Priya wants:

- Revenue at the **actual selling price after discount**
- Discount given
- Gross sales, returns, and net sales shown separately

The transcript does **not** define whether “gross sales” means:

1. sales before returns but **after discounts**, or  
2. sales before both returns and discounts.

The model supports both as long as `discount_amount` is captured:

- **Sales after discount, before returns:** sum of positive `actual_line_revenue`
- **Sales before discount, before returns:** positive `actual_line_revenue + discount_amount`
- **Net sales:** sales after discount less returns

The business definition of the label **gross sales** should be agreed before reporting is accepted.

---

### Dimension tables

#### `DimDate`

**Grain:** One calendar date.

**Key attributes:**

- `date_key`
- Full date
- Day
- Week identifier / week start date
- Month
- Month name
- Quarter, if used
- Year

**Purpose:** Supports daily analysis, arbitrary date ranges, year-on-year comparison, and week-on-week comparison. A week identifier must remain independent of month assignment so that weeks crossing month boundaries can be reported consistently.

---

#### `DimStore`

**Grain:** One store.

**Key attributes:**

- `store_key`
- Store identifier
- Store name, if available
- Region identifier/name
- Store format:
  - High street
  - Retail park
  - Concession

**Purpose:** Supports sales analysis by store, region, format, and the whole estate. Region and format are retained in the store dimension to preserve a star-schema structure.

---

#### `DimProduct`

**Grain:** One sellable product/item.

**Key attributes:**

- `product_key`
- Product/item identifier
- Product category

**Purpose:** Supports the requested analysis of sales and promotions by product category. The fact remains at item/receipt-line level even though category is the stated reporting requirement.

---

#### `DimLoyaltySegment`

**Grain:** One loyalty status segment.

**Key attributes:**

- `loyalty_segment_key`
- Loyalty segment:
  - Member
  - Guest

**Purpose:** Supports segmentation of transactions and sales between loyalty members and guests.

---

#### `DimTransactionType`

**Grain:** One transaction-line type.

**Key attributes:**

- `transaction_type_key`
- Transaction type:
  - Sale
  - Return

**Purpose:** Allows sales and returns to be reported separately while remaining in the same receipt-line fact table.

---

#### `DimPromotion`

**Grain:** One promotion.

**Key attributes:**

- `promotion_key`
- Promotion identifier
- Promotion name/description, if available
- Promotion type, such as:
  - Buy-one-get-one
  - Percentage-off
- Promotion start date
- Promotion end date

**Purpose:** Identifies promotions and their active windows for promotion analysis.

---

### Promotion bridge tables

Promotions may need to be associated with receipt lines and, where applicable, product categories.

#### `BridgeReceiptLinePromotion`

**Grain:** One association between one receipt line and one promotion.

**Key attributes/measures:**

- `receipt_line_key`
- `promotion_key`
- Promotion-attributed discount amount, where the source records it

**Purpose:** Allows the model to identify which sale lines were subject to a promotion. A bridge avoids assuming that only one promotion can apply to a receipt line.

---

#### `BridgePromotionProductCategory` — where category applicability is recorded

**Grain:** One promotion-to-product-category association.

**Key attributes:**

- `promotion_key`
- `product_key` or product-category identifier

**Purpose:** Supports category-based promotions such as percentage-off-category promotions. It also supports analysis of category sales during a promotion window, even where not every sale line has a promotion directly applied.

---

### Where returns fit

Returns are not netted away in a separate aggregate. They are stored as rows in `FactRetailReceiptLine` with `transaction_type = Return`.

This permits reporting of:

- Gross sales
- Returns
- Net sales
- Units sold
- Units returned

by any supported dimension, including date, store, region, store format, product category, promotion, and loyalty segment.

---

### Where promotions fit

Promotions fit through:

1. `DimPromotion`, which records promotion type and active dates.
2. `BridgeReceiptLinePromotion`, which links promotions to affected receipt lines where available.
3. `BridgePromotionProductCategory`, where a promotion is applicable to one or more categories.

The sales fact remains the source for promotion effectiveness analysis. No separate “promotion effectiveness fact” is required because sales during a promotion window and sales during a baseline period can be calculated from the same receipt-line fact.

---

### Scope limitation supported by the transcript

Priya wants to know whether promotions lifted sales or “gave away margin,” but the transcript provides no cost, margin, or cost-of-goods data. Therefore, this model supports:

- Sales during promotion versus baseline
- Discount given
- Returns
- Net sales

It does **not** support an actual margin or profitability calculation without additional cost data not mentioned in the interview.

---

## 2. Functional Requirements

1. **The solution shall retain and report three years of sales and return history.**  
   Acceptance test: Users can select and analyse dates covering up to the prior three years of available history.

2. **The solution shall refresh sales and return data daily.**  
   Acceptance test: Transactions from the prior trading day are available after the daily refresh; real-time reporting is not required.

3. **The solution shall retain sales and return data at individual receipt-line level.**  
   Acceptance test: A user can inspect a reported result down to receipt number, receipt line, product/item, quantity, actual selling price after discount, and transaction type.

4. **The solution shall provide sales analysis for any selected date range, including a single day.**  
   Acceptance test: A user can run sales analysis for one specified date, a week, a month, or another selected date range.

5. **The solution shall report units sold, revenue at actual selling price after discount, and discount amount.**  
   Acceptance test: For a selected date range and reporting slice, the output displays units sold, actual sales revenue, and discount given.

6. **The solution shall allow sales results to be grouped and filtered by store, region, and product category.**  
   Acceptance test: For the same selected date range, a user can view results by store, by region, by product category, and combinations of those attributes.

7. **The solution shall support comparison of stores by store format.**  
   Acceptance test: A user can filter or group store performance by high street, retail park, and concession formats.

8. **The solution shall show gross sales, returns, and net sales as separate values rather than netting returns invisibly into sales.**  
   Acceptance test: For a selected date range, store, region, or product category, the output displays separate gross-sales, returns, and net-sales values.

9. **The solution shall support week-on-week and year-on-year comparisons.**  
   Acceptance test: For a selected reporting period, the output can show the corresponding prior-week and prior-year comparison values.

10. **The solution shall support weekly reporting where a week spans a month boundary.**  
    Acceptance test: A week crossing two calendar months is reported as one consistent week and is not split or assigned inconsistently because of the month boundary.

11. **The solution shall support promotion effectiveness analysis by product category.**  
    Acceptance test: For a selected promotion and product category, the output shows category sales during the promotion window and category sales for a designated comparable non-promotion baseline period.

12. **The solution shall make promotion discount amounts visible for promotion analysis where a promotion can be associated with a receipt line.**  
    Acceptance test: For a selected promotion, the output can show the discount amount associated with that promotion’s sales lines.

13. **The comparable normal-period baseline used for promotion effectiveness shall be defined before acceptance of the promotion report.**  
    Acceptance test: Each promotion-effectiveness result identifies the normal-period baseline used for comparison. The transcript requires a “comparable normal period” but does not define the comparison method.

14. **The solution shall allow sales analysis to be segmented between loyalty members and guests.**  
    Acceptance test: For any selected date range and supported reporting slice, a user can display sales separately for loyalty-member transactions and guest transactions.

15. **Loyalty segmentation shall be treated as lower priority than the three stated launch priorities.**  
    The launch priorities explicitly stated by Priya are:
    - Sales by store, region, and product category over any date range
    - Promotion effectiveness versus a baseline
    - Returns visible separately from gross sales

    Loyalty member-versus-guest segmentation is requested but described as “the cherry on top,” rather than one of the three launch priorities.

gpt-5.6-sol.text:
## 1. Dimensional data model

### Fact table: `FactRetailTransactionLine`

**Grain:** One row per merchandise item line on a sale or return receipt. A line may represent multiple units through its quantity measure.

**Keys**
- `DateKey`
- `StoreKey`
- `ProductKey`
- `CategoryKey`
- `PromotionKey` — nullable when no promotion is applied
- `LoyaltySegmentKey`
- `LineEventTypeKey`
- Receipt/transaction identifier — degenerate dimension
- Receipt line number — degenerate dimension

**Measures**
- `SaleQuantity`
- `ReturnQuantity`
- `ActualUnitSalePriceAfterDiscount` — non-additive
- `LineSalesRevenue` — actual post-discount revenue for sale lines
- `LineReturnAmount` — positive refunded amount for return lines
- `LineDiscountAmount`

**Calculated measures**
- **Units sold** = sum of `SaleQuantity`
- **Gross sales** = sum of `LineSalesRevenue`, excluding returns
- **Returns** = sum of `LineReturnAmount`
- **Net sales** = gross sales minus returns
- **Discount given** = sum of `LineDiscountAmount`
- **Transaction count** = distinct count of receipt/transaction identifier

Here, gross sales means sales revenue at the actual post-discount selling price, before subtracting returns. It does not mean pre-discount ticket value.

### Factless fact table: `FactPromotionCategoryDay`

**Grain:** One row per promotion, applicable product category, and active calendar date.

**Keys**
- `PromotionKey`
- `CategoryKey`
- `DateKey`

**Measures:** None.

This table identifies the category and date window covered by each promotion. It allows all category sales during the promotion window—not only lines explicitly carrying the promotion—to be compared with sales in a designated normal period.

### Dimensions

#### `DimDate`
Key attributes:
- `DateKey`
- Calendar date
- Day
- Calendar week identifier
- Week start date
- Week end date
- Month
- Calendar year

Week and month are independent attributes so a week spanning two months remains one reporting week.

#### `DimStore`
Key attributes:
- `StoreKey`
- Store business identifier
- Store name
- Store format: high street, retail park, concession
- Region identifier
- Region name
- Estate name/identifier

Hierarchy: **Estate → Region → Store**. Store format is a separate comparison attribute.

#### `DimProduct`
Key attributes:
- `ProductKey`
- Product business identifier
- Product name

#### `DimProductCategory`
Key attributes:
- `CategoryKey`
- Category business identifier
- Category name

#### `DimPromotion`
Key attributes:
- `PromotionKey`
- Promotion identifier
- Promotion name
- Promotion type/mechanic, such as buy-one-get-one or percentage-off
- Promotion start date
- Promotion end date

#### `DimLoyaltySegment`
Key attributes:
- `LoyaltySegmentKey`
- Loyalty status: member or guest

The transcript supports member-versus-guest analysis, not an individual customer/member dimension.

#### `DimLineEventType`
Key attributes:
- `LineEventTypeKey`
- Event type: sale or return

### Placement of returns and promotions

- **Returns:** Stored at line level in `FactRetailTransactionLine` and identified as return events. Return amounts are retained separately and are not netted into sales during ingestion.
- **Promotions:** A nullable `PromotionKey` on the transaction line identifies directly applied promotions. `FactPromotionCategoryDay` represents the overall category/window affected by a promotion for baseline comparisons.
- **Margin limitation:** No cost or cost-of-goods data was mentioned. The model can compare units, revenue, and discount given, but cannot calculate actual gross margin or profit.

---

## 2. Functional requirements

1. **The solution shall retain transaction data at individual receipt-line grain.** Each line shall expose its receipt identifier, line number, date, store, product/category, quantity, actual post-discount selling price, discount amount, and sale-or-return status.

2. **The solution shall calculate units sold, sales revenue, and discount given from receipt-line data.** Revenue shall use the price actually charged after discount rather than an undiscounted price.

3. **Launch priority: Users shall be able to report sales by store, region, and product category for any selected date range.** The selected range may be as short as a single day.

4. **Users shall be able to roll store results up through region to the whole estate.** They shall also be able to group or compare stores by format: high street, retail park, and concession.

5. **Launch priority: The solution shall display gross sales, returns, and net sales as separate values.** Net sales shall equal gross sales minus returns; returns shall not be hidden by pre-netting them into sales.

6. **Returns shall be visible at daily grain and by the same store, region, and product-category dimensions as sales.** A large return on a particular day must therefore be identifiable separately from that day’s gross sales.

7. **Launch priority: Users shall be able to compare category sales during a selected promotion window with sales for the same category during a designated comparable normal period.** The comparison shall show at least units sold and revenue, with discount given shown for assessing promotion cost.

8. **Promotion reporting shall distinguish promotion mechanics and promotion windows.** Users shall be able to identify promotions such as buy-one-get-one and percentage-off promotions and analyze the categories and dates to which they applied.

9. **Users shall be able to segment transaction and sales results between loyalty members and guests.** This shall include distinct transaction counts so the share of member versus guest transactions can be measured. This is a lower launch priority than the three items Priya explicitly prioritized.

10. **The solution shall provide at least three years of historical data for trend analysis.**

11. **The solution shall support week-on-week comparison and comparison with the same period in the prior year.**

12. **Calendar weeks shall remain intact when they cross a month boundary.** A week spanning two months shall not be split into separate weekly totals merely because the month changes.

13. **Data shall be refreshed daily.** Real-time updating is not required.

14. **The comparable-normal-period rule for promotion analysis shall be explicitly selectable or configured.** The transcript does not define an automatic method for choosing a “comparable” baseline, so no matching algorithm should be assumed.

gpt-5.6-luna.text:
## 1. Dimensional data model — star schema

### Fact table

#### `FactRetailLine`

**Grain:** one recorded product line on one sale or return transaction, for one store and one transaction/event date.

A transaction containing three different items produces three fact rows. A return is represented as a return line rather than being netted into the original sale.

**Key fields**

- `date_key`
- `store_key`
- `product_key`
- `promotion_key` — nullable/no-promotion value where applicable
- `loyalty_status_key`
- `transaction_id` — degenerate dimension for the receipt/transaction
- `transaction_line_number`
- `transaction_type` — `Sale` or `Return`

**Measures**

- `quantity`
- `actual_unit_price_after_discount`
- `gross_sales_amount` — positive sale value before returns, using the actual selling price after discounts
- `discount_amount`
- `return_units`
- `return_amount` — stored as a positive amount
- `net_sales_amount` — derived as `gross_sales_amount - return_amount`
- `distinct_transaction_count` — derived from `transaction_id`, not summed across line rows

**Returns**

Returns are recorded in the same fact table with `transaction_type = 'Return'`. Return units and return value are kept separately from gross sales. This supports reporting:

```text
Net sales = Gross sales - Return amount
```

The return date should be the date of the return event, since Priya wants returns to be visible as activity affecting a particular day.

**Promotions**

A sale line may reference a promotion through `promotion_key`. The fact retains the discount amount independently of the promotion reference, since not every discount is necessarily proven to be promotion-related. Promotion-period and baseline comparisons are derived using the fact date, product category, and promotion attributes.

No margin measure is included because the transcript provides no cost or margin data.

---

### Dimension tables

#### `DimDate`

Key attributes:

- `date_key`
- `calendar_date`
- `day_of_week`
- `week_key`
- `week_start_date`
- `week_end_date`
- `month_key`
- `month_name`
- `quarter`
- `year`

The week attributes must be assigned independently of the month so that a week spanning two months remains one week for week-on-week reporting.

#### `DimStore`

Key attributes:

- `store_key`
- `store_id`
- `store_name`
- `store_format` — for example, high street, retail park, or concession
- `region_key`

#### `DimRegion`

Key attributes:

- `region_key`
- `region_name`
- `estate_key` or `estate_name`

This represents the hierarchy:

```text
Store → Region → Whole estate
```

#### `DimProduct`

Key attributes:

- `product_key`
- `product_id`
- `product_name`
- `category_key`
- `category_name`

Product category is retained in the product dimension so sales can be sliced by category without requiring a separate snowflaked category table.

#### `DimPromotion`

Key attributes:

- `promotion_key`
- `promotion_id`
- `promotion_name`
- `promotion_type` — for example, buy-one-get-one or percentage-off
- `promotion_start_date`
- `promotion_end_date`
- `category_scope`

A no-promotion member/value should be available for lines with no associated promotion.

#### `DimLoyaltyStatus`

Key attributes:

- `loyalty_status_key`
- `loyalty_status` — `Member` or `Guest`

No customer-level dimension is specified by the transcript; the requested segmentation is only member versus guest.

---

## 2. Functional requirements

1. **Line-level sales capture**  
   The system shall retain one record for each product line on a sale transaction, including transaction ID, line number, date, store, product, quantity, actual selling price after discount, and discount amount.  
   **Test:** A receipt containing three item lines produces three line-level records with the correct quantities and prices.

2. **Sales reporting by core business dimensions** `[Launch]`  
   Users shall be able to report units sold, gross sales, discount amount, and net sales by store, region, and product category for any selected date range.  
   **Test:** A user can select an arbitrary start and end date and return results grouped by each of store, region, and category.

3. **Single-day analysis**  
   Users shall be able to select a single calendar day and view its sales and return measures without relying on a weekly or monthly total.  
   **Test:** Selecting one date returns only activity dated on that date.

4. **Store and regional hierarchy**  
   Every store shall be associated with one region, and regional results shall be aggregable to a whole-estate total.  
   **Test:** A store-level total rolls up to its region, and all regions roll up to the estate total without unassigned stores or regions.

5. **Like-for-like comparison by store format**  
   Users shall be able to filter and compare results by store format, including high street, retail park, and concession.  
   **Test:** A report can return sales for only one format and can compare two or more formats over the same date range.

6. **Separate return reporting** `[Launch]`  
   The system shall show gross sales, returns, and net sales as separate measures rather than presenting only a netted result.  
   **Test:** For a period containing 100 of gross sales and 25 of returns, the report shows gross sales of 100, returns of 25, and net sales of 75.

7. **Return visibility by date and business dimensions**  
   Users shall be able to analyze return units and return value by date, store, region, product category, and—where available—loyalty status and promotion.  
   **Test:** A return occurring on a selected day appears in that day’s return measures and does not reduce the gross-sales measure.

8. **Promotion information capture** `[Launch]`  
   Sales lines associated with a promotion shall retain the promotion identifier, promotion type, promotion window, applicable category scope, and discount amount.  
   **Test:** A buy-one-get-one or percentage-off promotion can be identified and its associated sales and discounts can be isolated.

9. **Promotion-period versus baseline comparison** `[Launch]`  
   Users shall be able to compare sales for a selected product category during a promotion window with a selected comparable normal baseline period. The comparison shall show at least units, gross sales, discount amount, and net sales for both periods.  
   **Test:** Given a promotion window and a baseline date range, the report displays both periods side by side and provides the difference between them.  
   **Scope note:** The transcript does not specify an automatic method for determining what constitutes a “comparable” baseline, so the baseline-selection rule must be defined separately rather than invented in the model.

10. **Loyalty segmentation**  
    Users shall be able to segment sales, returns, units, revenue, and discount measures between loyalty members and guests.  
    **Test:** A report filtered to `Member` and a report filtered to `Guest` return separate, non-overlapping results.

11. **Three years of history**  
    The reporting solution shall provide three years of sales and return history for trend and comparison analysis.  
    **Test:** A user can query a date range extending up to three years into the past, subject to the available source data.

12. **Daily data refresh**  
    The reporting data shall be refreshed daily rather than requiring real-time availability.  
    **Test:** Each scheduled daily refresh makes the prior day’s available sales and return records queryable.

13. **Year-on-year comparison**  
    Users shall be able to compare the same calendar period across different years.  
    **Test:** Selecting a period in one year returns the corresponding period in another year with comparable sales, return, discount, and unit measures.

14. **Week-on-week comparison across month boundaries**  
    Users shall be able to compare one week with the preceding week, with weeks remaining intact even when a week spans two calendar months.  
    **Test:** A week containing dates from two months is reported as one week in weekly results and is not split solely because of the month boundary.

kimi-k3.text:
# 1. Dimensional Data Model

## Fact table

**FACT_SALES_LINE**
- **Grain: one row per individual item line on a customer receipt** (Priya: "down to the individual line on a receipt... each item has a quantity and the price it actually sold at after any discount"). Return lines live in the same table (see returns note below).
- **Degenerate identifiers:** receipt number, receipt line number (needed to reassemble multi-item transactions).
- **Foreign keys:** date_key, store_key, product_key, promotion_key (defaults to a "No promotion" member), loyalty_key, line_type_key.
- **Measures:**
  - `quantity` (signed)
  - `line_sales_amount` — actual sold price after discount × quantity (signed)
  - `discount_amount` (signed)
- **Derived measures:** gross sales (line_type = Sale), returns value (line_type = Return), net sales (gross − returns).

**FACT_PROMOTION_COVERAGE (factless)**
- **Grain: one row per promotion per affected category per day.** Defines *when* each promotion ran and *which categories* it covered — required to answer "sales for a category during a promotion window versus a comparable normal period."

## Dimension tables

| Dimension | Key attributes | Source in transcript |
|---|---|---|
| DIM_DATE | calendar_date, day_of_week, **week_id (not split at month boundaries)**, month, year | YoY and WoW comparison; "some weeks span a month boundary" |
| DIM_STORE | store_id, store_name, region, format (high street / retail park / concession) | "store → region → whole estate"; format for like-for-like |
| DIM_PRODUCT | product_id, product_name, category | slice by product category |
| DIM_PROMOTION | promotion_id, name, type (BOGO, % off category, etc.), start_date, end_date | "buy-one-get-one, percentage off a category" |
| DIM_LOYALTY | segment (member / guest) | "segment by member versus guest" — no customer attributes were requested |
| DIM_LINE_TYPE | type (sale / return) | returns visible separately |

## Where returns and promotions fit

- **Returns:** modeled as receipt lines in FACT_SALES_LINE with line_type = Return and negative quantity/amount. This makes net sales fall out naturally while letting gross sales and returns be reported as separate measures — directly fixing "returns are just netted off somewhere... invisible."
- **Promotions:** two hooks. (1) `promotion_key` + `discount_amount` on discounted fact lines captures how much discount each promotion gave away. (2) FACT_PROMOTION_COVERAGE defines the window and category scope for lift-vs-baseline analysis. *Note:* Priya said "gave away margin," but cost/margin data was never discussed — discount_amount is the supportable proxy; margin would be a follow-up question.

**Explicitly not modeled** (not in transcript): inventory, cost/margin, customer demographics, sales channel, time-of-day.

---

# 2. Functional Requirements

Tagged **[Launch]** per Priya's "if you could only have three things"; **[Later]** = her "cherry on top."

1. **[Launch] Receipt-line capture.** The system shall store one record per item line per receipt, including quantity and the actual unit selling price after any discount. *Test: a 3-item receipt produces 3 lines whose amounts reconcile to the receipt total.*

2. **[Launch] Flexible sales slicing.** Users shall report units sold and revenue by store, region, and product category over any user-selected date range. *Test: an arbitrary range grouped by region × category reconciles to the line-level sum.*

3. **[Launch] Single-day drill-down.** Users shall isolate any individual trading day. *Test: filtering to one date returns exactly that day's lines.*

4. **[Launch] Discount visibility.** Discount given shall be captured per line and reportable as a total by store, category, promotion, and date. *Test: reported discount for a slice equals the sum of line-level discount_amount.*

5. **[Launch] Gross / returns / net separation.** Every sales view shall show gross sales, returns, and net sales as three distinct measures. *Test: on a day containing a large return, gross is unaffected, returns shows the spike, and net = gross − returns.*

6. **[Launch] Promotion effectiveness vs. baseline.** For a selected promotion, users shall compare units and revenue for the affected category during the promotion window against a user-selected comparable prior period, alongside total discount given during the window. *Test: window dates auto-populate from the promotion; the baseline period is user-selectable; lift is computed and displayed.*

7. **[Launch] Store hierarchy and format.** Sales shall roll up store → region → estate without loss, and users shall filter/compare by store format. *Test: store totals sum exactly to region and estate totals; filtering by "concession" returns only concession stores.*

8. **[Launch] Year-on-year comparison.** Users shall compare any period to the equivalent period a year earlier. *Test: selecting a range returns aligned prior-year figures for the same calendar dates.*

9. **[Launch] Week-on-week with month-spanning weeks.** Weeks shall be treated as intact 7-day periods even when crossing a month boundary. *Test: a week spanning e.g. 30 Sep–6 Oct appears as one bucket and compares correctly to the prior week and prior-year equivalent week.*

10. **[Launch] Three years of history.** At least 36 months of sales history shall be loaded and queryable at full line grain. *Test: earliest queryable date is ≥ 3 years back.*

11. **[Launch] Daily refresh.** The prior trading day's data shall be available each day; real-time is not required. *Test: yesterday's receipts are present by the agreed morning cutoff.*

12. **[Later] Loyalty segmentation.** Every transaction shall be classified member or guest, and all measures shall be filterable/comparable by that segment. *Test: member + guest totals equal overall totals for any slice.* Deliberately phased after launch per Priya's own prioritization.

haiku-4-5.text:
# Data Quality Assessment Report

## COMPLETENESS
Missing or null values that prevent full understanding of customer records.

| Issue | Affected Row(s) | Problem | Severity | Remediation |
